The EU’s position followed tensions with the Trump administration, including threats to assert control over Greenland, a Danish territory. Speaking after closed-door meetings at the International Energy Agency (IEA) in Paris, Wright defended the United States as a dependable energy partner, describing it as a “rock-solid supplier” that has never used energy exports as a political weapon. He emphasized that U.S. energy exports are conducted by private companies operating under binding contracts, rather than being directed by the government for geopolitical leverage. Wright also argued that European countries have undermined their own energy security

item.title: David Campbell convicted of murdering former colleague Brian Low in Perthshire
item.description: David Campbell, 77, has been found guilty of murdering his former colleague Brian Low, 65, in February 2024. The killing occurred on a remote track near Aberfeldy, Perthshire, and Campbell fled the scene using his wife's electric bike. The court heard that Campbell had planned the murder carefully, disabling his home CCTV, covering his doorbell camera, and disposing of the shotgun used in the crime. The High Court in Glasgow found him guilty despite his claims of being at home at the time of the shooting. Both men had previously worked at Edradynate Estate, where Campbell was head gamekeeper and Low was a groundsman. Evidence included CCTV footage, soil samples linking the bike to the crime scene, and testimony about Campbell's animosity toward Low. Police Scotland faced criticism for initially treating the death as a medical incident, delaying the forensic investigation. Campbell denied malicious intent, but witnesses described a longstanding feud, and he admitted spreading false accu

item.title: Prince Harry and Meghan Markle visit Jordan to support refugee programs
item.description: The Duke and Duchess of Sussex have arrived in Jordan for a two-day visit aimed at supporting refugees in the region. Their trip began with a roundtable summit hosted by the World Health Organization (WHO) in Amman, attended by United Nations representatives, diplomats from multiple countries, and donors. WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus welcomed the couple personally. During the visit, the Sussexes will meet Jordanian leaders, health officials, and WHO teams, as well as visit frontline health and mental health programs. They are also scheduled to see World Central Kitchen staff coordinating food relief for Gaza and visit Questscope's youth centre, a social development organization. The visit occurs amid the Royal Family facing scrutiny following the recent arrest of Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor on suspicion of misconduct in public office. British Ambassador to Jordan, Philip Hall, expressed appreciation for the couple's presence and highlighted the ongoing challenges face

item.title: Switzerland Announces Financial Support for Victims of Crans-Montana Bar Fire
item.description: The Swiss Federal Council has announced a one-off payment of 50,000 Swiss francs (£47,755) to each severely injured survivor and bereaved family member affected by the New Year bar fire in Crans-Montana, which killed 41 people and injured 115. The fire is believed to have started when sparkling candles ignited foam soundproofing in the bar's basement ceiling. Many victims were teenagers and foreigners, including citizens from France and Italy. The payment, described as a 'solidarity contribution,' is intended to provide immediate financial assistance and demonstrate compassion. In addition, the Swiss government plans to allocate up to 20 million francs to facilitate out-of-court settlements between victims, insurers, and authorities, potentially avoiding lengthy legal disputes. Parliament is being urged to expedite legislation to help victims, while 8.5 million francs will be set aside to support cantons in managing costs. These measures follow an analysis by the Federal Office of Just

item.title: UK Survey Reveals Household Energy Habits That Often Lead to Arguments
item.description: A recent survey conducted by Uswitch.com highlights the energy habits in UK households that most frequently spark arguments among family members. The study found that leaving lights on in empty rooms is the top source of disputes, with 44% of households reporting disagreements over this behavior. Other common triggers include leaving electronic devices on unnecessarily and allowing the television to remain on when no one is watching, which cause disagreements in about 40% of homes. Heating costs and thermostat settings are another significant area of contention, with 39% of households arguing over the thermostat temperature. Among couples, over a third (36%) specifically argue about the temperature their thermostat is set to. Some individuals have even admitted to secretly adjusting the thermostat without their partner's knowledge, with 19% confessing to this behavior. Energy bills themselves are a notable source of tension, with 31% of respondents reporting disagreements over gas and

item.title: Details of the villa where Jalisco cartel leader 'El Mencho' was killed
item.description: Nemesio Ruben Oseguera Cervantes, widely known as 'El Mencho', leader of the powerful Jalisco New Generation Cartel, spent his final hours in a luxury villa in the Tapalpa Country Club, Jalisco, before being killed in a Mexican military operation. The villa, a two-floor home with stone walls and a red-tiled roof, featured spacious rooms, modern finishes, and large windows. Inside, authorities found a mix of personal items, including neatly folded clothes, skincare products, takeaway food containers, and medications for various health issues such as migraines, insomnia, and acid reflux. Vials of Tationil Plus, an antioxidant, were stored in a freezer alongside a dosage schedule. Religious items, including figurines of the Virgin of Guadalupe and Saint Jude Thaddeus, were arranged on a makeshift altar, accompanied by a handwritten Bible passage expressing trust in God. During the operation, Oseguera Cervantes attempted to escape through the villa's back garden but was shot and later died

item.title: CPJ reports unprecedented number of journalist deaths in 2025, with majority attributed to Israeli military actions
item.description:
The Committee to Protect Journalists (CPJ) reported that 2025 was the deadliest year for journalists since it began collecting data more than three decades ago, with a record 129 press members killed worldwide. According to CPJ, two-thirds of these deaths were attributed to Israeli military actions, marking the second consecutive year of record-breaking fatalities linked largely to the Israel-Gaza conflict. Of the 86 journalists killed by Israeli fire, more than 60% were Palestinians reporting from Gaza. CPJ documented 47 cases classified as targeted killings, the highest number of deliberate killings in a decade, and stated that Israel was responsible for 81% of those cases.
The report highlights that over three-quarters of all journalist deaths occurred in conflict zones, with additional fatalities reported in Sudan and Ukraine. Drone strikes emerged as a significant factor, with 39 journalists killed by drones in 2025 compared to just two in 2023. CPJ notes that many of these atta

item.title: Man Sentenced to Prison for Selling Counterfeit Aircraft Engine Parts with Forged Safety Certificates
item.description:
Jose Alejandro Zamora Yrala, a 38-year-old former techno DJ, has been sentenced to four years and eight months in prison for selling nearly £7 million worth of counterfeit aircraft engine parts to major airlines. Operating from his home garage in Virginia Water, Surrey, he ran a company called AOG Technics, which supplied more than 60,000 engine components between 2019 and 2023. These parts, primarily for CFM56 engines used in Airbus A320 and Boeing 737 aircraft, were accompanied by forged Authorised Release Certificates (ARCs) that falsely guaranteed their airworthiness.
Investigators revealed that Zamora Yrala altered genuine safety certificates using his home computer and created fake shipment documents to make it appear that the parts had been purchased directly from legitimate manufacturers. The fraudulent scheme generated over £7.7 million in revenue, with approximately 90% derived from unlawful activity.
The deception came to light when a bolt supplied to Portuguese airline TA

item.title: UK Children’s Weekly Pocket Money Rises 8% in 2025, With Regional Differences
item.description:
Children across the UK received an above-inflation increase in pocket money in 2025, according to new data from GoHenry, a financial education app designed to help young people manage money. The average weekly pocket money payment rose to £10.73, marking an 8% increase from the 2024 average of £9.92. In total, children earned £195 million over the course of the year.
The data also revealed notable regional differences. Children in the South East received the highest average weekly payments at £14.30, representing a sharp 44.87% rise compared with the previous year. In contrast, children in the east of England received the lowest average amount at £8.88, which was actually a decrease from 2024 levels.
Chores remain a key way for children to earn money. The most commonly completed task was tidying bedrooms, earning an average of £1.12 per task. However, the highest-paying chore was looking after plants, with children earning an average of £1.91 for this responsibility.
When it comes t

item.title: Pennsylvania Box Huckleberry Colony Estimated to Be Up to 13,000 Years Old
item.description:
A colony of box huckleberry shrubs in rural Pennsylvania is considered one of the oldest living organisms on Earth, with scientists estimating its age at up to 13,000 years. The colony, known as Losh Run, grows near the Juniata River close to Route 322/22. Although each individual stem of the plant stands only about a foot tall, the colony spreads horizontally across the forest floor by producing offshoots, forming a large, genetically identical clonal organism.
Unlike trees, whose age can be determined by counting growth rings, shrubs such as the box huckleberry cannot be dated so directly. Instead, researchers estimate the colony’s age by measuring its total size and applying its average growth rate of approximately six inches per year. Based on this method, scientists believe the plant began growing near the end of the last Ice Age, meaning it has survived dramatic climatic shifts and thousands of years of environmental change.
Despite its resilience, the Losh Run colony suffered

item.title: Heavy rainfall in Spain and Morocco disrupts UK fruit and vegetable supplies
item.description:
UK supermarkets are experiencing sporadic shortages of certain fruits and vegetables due to severe weather conditions in Spain and Morocco, two key suppliers of fresh produce to the British market. Strawberries, raspberries, avocados and peppers have been identified as the most affected items, according to analysis of Assosia data reported by The Grocer.
Parts of southern Spain and Morocco have endured weeks of torrential rain, which has significantly disrupted agricultural production. Spain’s meteorological agency, AEMET, recorded the highest rainfall levels for January since 2001. The persistent downpours have raised concerns about the viability of farms in affected regions, with some crops reportedly submerged by flooding. In Morocco, storm conditions in December also caused logistical disruptions, further complicating the supply chain.
Industry experts indicate that the full extent of the damage is not yet known, but Spanish farmers have already reported losses amounting to milli

item.title: Man sentenced to life imprisonment with 25-year minimum for the murder of nine-year-old Lilia Valutyte in Boston
item.description: Deividas Skebas, a 26-year-old Lithuanian man, has been sentenced to life imprisonment with a minimum term of 25 years for the murder of nine-year-old Lilia Valutyte. The tragic incident occurred on July 28, 2022, in Boston, Lincolnshire, where Lilia was playing with a hula hoop on Fountain Lane outside the embroidery shop where her mother, Lina Savicke, worked. Skebas approached the unsuspecting girl and stabbed her once in the chest with a knife, leading to her death within an hour despite efforts by an off-duty police officer, paramedics, and doctors. CCTV footage captured Lilia as a carefree and happy child moments before the random attack. Skebas admitted to killing her but denied murder, arguing diminished responsibility due to his mental health condition. Three psychiatrists confirmed he suffered from schizophrenia and exhibited psychotic symptoms on the day of the crime, including delusions of being controlled by a microchip. After an eight-day trial at Lincoln Crown Court, the

item.title: Tourette’s campaigner criticises BBC and Bafta after involuntary slur aired during awards ceremony
item.description:
John Davidson, a Tourette’s campaigner from Galashiels, Scotland, has criticised the BBC for broadcasting an involuntary racial slur he shouted during the Bafta Film Awards ceremony. Davidson, whose condition involves uncontrollable verbal tics, called out offensive words, including a racial slur, while actors Michael B Jordan and Delroy Lindo were on stage. He argued that the BBC and Bafta should have taken greater precautions, particularly given prior discussions about the likelihood of his tics during the event. Davidson questioned why he was seated near a microphone, suggesting that organisers knew of his condition and should have anticipated potential issues.
The BBC apologised, stating that the offensive language resulted from involuntary verbal tics associated with Tourette syndrome and that the slur was broadcast in error. The corporation removed the segment from BBC iPlayer and confirmed that another slur had been edited out before transmission. According to BBC News, produce

item.title: Workday Forecasts Slower Revenue Growth Despite CEO’s Optimism About Agentic AI
item.description:
Workday CEO Aneel Bhusri used the company’s fourth-quarter earnings call—his first since returning to the role—to emphasize confidence in the company’s strategic direction, particularly its push into agentic AI. Bhusri highlighted Workday’s two decades of experience managing HR and financial data, arguing that this foundation gives the company a competitive advantage of five to seven years over leading AI rivals. He stressed that Workday’s core HR and financial applications remain strong and continue to grow, providing a stable base upon which to build AI-driven solutions. The company aims to adopt a consumption-based model that allows it to benefit financially whether applications are built internally or by third parties on top of its platform.
Despite this optimistic messaging, investors reacted negatively to Workday’s financial outlook. The company forecast subscription revenue growth of 12 to 13 percent for the fiscal year ending January 31, 2027, projecting revenue between $9.925

item.title: BBC Verify examines key claims from Trump’s record-length State of the Union address
item.description:
BBC Verify reviewed several major claims made by President Donald Trump during his longest-ever State of the Union address, which lasted one hour and 47 minutes. In his speech, Trump argued that the US economy is improving, inflation is falling rapidly, immigration has been curbed, and multiple international conflicts have been resolved under his leadership.
On inflation, data shows that while price growth has slowed to 2.4% in the year to January 2026, prices are still rising. Inflation peaked at 9.1% in June 2022 during Joe Biden’s presidency following Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. Grocery prices overall rose 2.1% over the past year. Beef prices increased 15% year-on-year despite a slight recent monthly drop, while egg prices fell 34%. Economists cited by BBC Verify noted that Trump’s 2025 tariffs may have added nearly one percentage point to consumer inflation.
Trump claimed gasoline prices were below $2.30 per gallon in most states, but national averages show prices at $2.95, wit

item.title: UK Commons Speaker Admits Passing Information to Police About Lord Mandelson's Possible Travel Plans Before His Arrest
item.description: Commons Speaker Sir Lindsay Hoyle confirmed in a statement to Parliament that he provided 'relevant' information to the Metropolitan Police regarding Lord Peter Mandelson in good faith, describing it as his duty. This appears to verify reports that he informed police the peer might be a flight risk after learning during a recent visit to the British Virgin Islands that Mandelson planned to travel there. Hoyle expressed regret that the details became public. Mandelson, a former senior Labour politician and briefly UK ambassador to the US in 2025, was arrested on Monday on suspicion of misconduct in public office. The investigation stems from allegations that, as a minister years ago, he shared market-sensitive government information with the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. Mandelson was detained in London, interviewed at Wandsworth police station, and released on bail early Tuesday, surrendering his passport as a condition. His lawyers described the arrest as unnecessary, c

item.title: Two Arrested in Cornwall Over Alleged £6m Packaging Waste Credit Fraud
item.description:
Two individuals have been arrested in connection with an investigation into alleged waste packaging fraud and money laundering, according to the Environment Agency (EA). A 47-year-old man and a 47-year-old woman were detained during an early morning operation in Penzance, Cornwall, carried out jointly by the EA and Devon and Cornwall Police. Both have since been released pending further investigation.
The arrests are linked to suspected fraud within the UK’s Packaging Producer Responsibility (PPR) system. Under this system, Environment Agency-accredited reprocessors issue credits for the amount of packaging waste they recycle. Packaging producers are legally required to purchase these credits to contribute toward the cost of recycling the waste they generate. Investigators believe the suspects sold more than £6m worth of recycling credits, but allege that a substantial portion of the claimed recycling activity did not actually occur. Authorities suspect that reprocessing figures were

item.title: Man Arrested for Carrying Weapons Including Axe into Manchester Mosque During Ramadan Prayers; Starmer Expresses Concern
item.description:
On Tuesday evening, February 24, 2026, a serious security incident occurred at Manchester Central Mosque in the Victoria Park area of south Manchester during Ramadan prayers. Two men entered the mosque around 8:40 pm: one described as a white man in his 40s and the other as a black male. Witnesses reported that the suspects were acting suspiciously and carrying offensive weapons, including an axe, along with other items such as a knife or hammer in bags. Mosque volunteers and worshippers quickly noticed the suspicious behavior, which prompted an immediate police response from Greater Manchester Police (GMP).
Officers arrived swiftly and arrested the white man in his 40s on suspicion of carrying an offensive weapon and possession of class B drugs. He remains in custody for questioning. The second man reportedly fled the scene before police arrival, and officers are working to locate him. Importantly, no threats were made during the incident, and no one was injured. GMP has explicitly s

item.title: Deividas Skebas sentenced to life imprisonment for the murder of nine-year-old Lilia Valutyte in Boston
item.description:
A 26-year-old man, Deividas Skebas, has been sentenced to life imprisonment with a minimum term of 25 years for the fatal stabbing of nine-year-old Lilia Valutyte in Boston, Lincolnshire. The attack occurred on 28 July 2022 while Lilia was playing with a hula hoop outside her mother’s embroidery shop. Skebas stabbed the child in the heart, causing fatal injuries. Lilia’s mother, Lina Savickiene, discovered her daughter seriously wounded and initially believed the hula hoop might have caused an accident. She called for help and attempted to stem the bleeding as her daughter became pale. An off-duty police officer assisted at the scene, but despite efforts to save her, Lilia died from her injuries.
Skebas, a Lithuanian national diagnosed with schizophrenia, had returned to the United Kingdom just weeks before the attack. He denied murder but admitted manslaughter on the grounds of diminished responsibility. In 2023, he was deemed mentally unfit to stand trial. However, following a reass

item.title: Norway's King Harald hospitalized in Tenerife due to infection and dehydration
item.description: King Harald V of Norway, the oldest living European monarch at 89, was hospitalized on Tuesday evening while on a holiday in Tenerife with his wife, Queen Sonja. The king fell ill due to an infection and dehydration shortly after celebrating his birthday on Saturday. Officials from Norway's Royal Palace have confirmed that King Harald is in good condition. Norwegian Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Stoere expressed his wishes for a swift recovery. The king, who has served as Norway's ceremonial head of state since 1991, is expected to be assessed by his personal doctor with an update on his health anticipated on Wednesday. This is not the first time the king has been hospitalized during travel; in 2024, he was treated in Malaysia for an infection and received a temporary pacemaker before returning to Norway for a permanent device implantation. Authorities are monitoring his condition closely to ensure his recovery continues smoothly.

item.title: UK Expected to See Mild Temperatures Up to 19°C Amid Winter Rain
item.description: Parts of the UK are set to experience unseasonably warm weather on Wednesday, with temperatures potentially reaching 19°C (66.2°F), making some areas warmer than typical holiday destinations such as Malta and Barcelona. This surge in warmth occurs towards the end of a winter marked by above-average rainfall over the past 30 years. Meteorologists forecast that most of the country will enjoy mild conditions, especially in the southeast, where sunshine has already brought temperatures up to 16.6°C (61.9°F) in places like Frittenden in Kent and High Beach in Essex. Temperatures are expected to rise further, with some areas possibly hitting 19°C. However, cooler weather will move in from Thursday as westerly winds bring clouds and rain, though temperatures will generally remain above average. The temporary warmth is driven by a southerly airflow caused by low pressure to the west and high pressure to the east. The Met Office emphasized that this does not indicate a permanent shift to settle

item.title: Trump Supporters Express Shock After Family Members Are Detained and Deported by ICE
item.description: Daylin Silber, a Spanish-English court interpreter in South Carolina, is grappling with the deportation of her brother, Rolando Torricella De La Portil, who was detained by ICE during a routine check-in in Miami. Both Daylin and Rolando are Cuban immigrants who were granted permanent U.S. residency decades ago. Despite serving three years in federal prison in the early 2000s for a past drug-related conviction, Rolando maintained a clean record for over two decades and complied with all immigration requirements. His wife, Susan, emphasized that he followed all legal procedures. The deportation came after months in crowded detention facilities, including a high-security center nicknamed “Alligator Alcatraz.” Another family friend, Eddy Acosta, with a similar past conviction, reported inhumane conditions in detention before being deported. Both men ultimately agreed to deportation and are now trying to rebuild their lives in Mexico, though Rolando expressed frustration with being relocate

item.title: Chris Hemsworth Reflects on Moving Back to Australia for Family Life
item.description: Chris Hemsworth has described relocating from Los Angeles to Australia as the best decision for his family. Speaking on the Smartless podcast, he explained that the move coincided with the birth of his twin sons, Sasha and Tristan, and that the family was not enjoying life in L.A. due to work demands and paparazzi attention. Hemsworth, along with his wife Elsa Pataky and their daughter India Rose, settled in Byron Bay, New South Wales, purchasing and renovating a $7 million estate into a six-bedroom home with a media room, gym, spa, and games room. The actor emphasized the benefits of this lifestyle change, noting that returning home from work feels like being on holiday, thanks to their large property, farm animals, and outdoor activities such as surfing and riding motorbikes. He also compared their current life to his childhood in Melbourne, where he and his brothers grew up surrounded by bushland and nature, which influenced his love for adventure and acting. Overall, Hemsworth high

item.title: Martin Short mourns the loss of his daughter Katherine at 42
item.description: Canadian actor Martin Short has announced the tragic death of his daughter, Katherine Short, at the age of 42. Katherine, the eldest of three children adopted by Short and his late wife Nancy Dolman, was a social worker who was known for the light and joy she brought to those around her. The Short family has expressed deep grief and requested privacy during this difficult time. Law enforcement sources reported that her death is being treated as an apparent suicide. Following this personal tragedy, Martin Short has postponed upcoming live shows with his comedy partner Steve Martin in Milwaukee and Minneapolis. Despite this difficult period, Short remains nominated for Best Male Actor in a Comedy Series at the Actor Awards for his role as Oliver Putnam in 'Only Murders in the Building.' Katherine's mother, Nancy Dolman, passed away from ovarian cancer in 2010. The family continues to cope with this profound loss, while fans and colleagues offer condolences and support.

item.title: Zara Larsson Says She Blocks Artists She Considers Abusers, Including Chris Brown, From Her Spotify
item.description:
Swedish pop singer Zara Larsson has stated that she blocks certain artists from appearing on her Spotify playlists, specifically those she considers to be abusers. The 28-year-old performer made the comments during a “Cheap Shots” segment with Cosmopolitan, where she was asked to name an artist who would never feature on one of her playlists. Larsson responded that there are several artists she has blocked on Spotify and explained that all of them are individuals she views as abusers. She specifically mentioned Chris Brown as an example, saying that his music would not appear on her playlists.
Chris Brown has faced multiple legal controversies throughout his career, most notably the 2009 domestic violence case involving fellow singer Rihanna. In the years since, he has also faced additional allegations and legal issues. Larsson’s remarks reflect her personal stance on separating herself from artists whose behavior she finds unacceptable.
The singer is known not only for her internati

item.title: Mexico assures safety for 2026 World Cup fans despite recent cartel violence
item.description: Mexico's government has reassured international visitors that attending the 2026 FIFA World Cup will be safe, despite recent violent incidents linked to organized crime. The reassurance comes after the military operation that led to the killing of Nemesio Ruben Oseguera Cervantes, head of the Jalisco New Generation Cartel, in Jalisco state. His death triggered days of retaliatory violence, resulting in approximately 70 deaths and disruption to transportation, including flight suspensions. Guadalajara, Mexico City, and Monterrey are confirmed as host cities, with Guadalajara set to host four matches and the other two cities hosting nine combined. President Claudia Sheinbaum emphasized that all safety measures are in place and that the situation is stabilizing. She affirmed that Mexico's security strategy remains focused on promoting peace rather than escalating violence. FIFA President Gianni Infantino expressed confidence in Mexico's preparations and stated that the organization is clo

item.title: Omar Yaghi develops solar-powered device to harvest 1,000 liters of water from air daily
item.description: Professor Omar Yaghi, a chemist at the University of California, Berkeley and 2025 Nobel Prize winner, has developed an innovative device capable of producing up to 1,000 liters of clean water daily from air with less than 20% humidity. The technology, created by Yaghi's company Atoco, relies on Metal-Organic Frameworks (MOFs), highly porous synthetic materials engineered at the molecular level to trap water molecules. The MOFs act like a 'super-sponge,' capturing moisture as air passes through the device. When heated using sunlight or low-grade thermal energy, the captured water is released and condensed into liquid form. Unlike conventional atmospheric water generators, Yaghi's system is off-grid and energy-efficient. The machine, roughly the size of a 20-foot shipping container, can be deployed in disaster-stricken regions or arid areas, providing immediate access to safe drinking water. Growing up in a refugee community in Jordan without running water inspired Yaghi’s commitment to

item.title: Analysis of Trump's Record and Policies in His Lengthy State of the Union Address
item.description: In this Americast episode, the team discusses former President Donald Trump's unusually long State of the Union address, which lasted nearly two hours, making it the longest in history. The speech focused on defending his administration’s achievements, presenting them as a 'turnaround for the ages,' while minimizing the impact of the Supreme Court’s ruling against his key tariff policy, despite the judges being present. Trump emphasized topics including the economy, immigration, Democratic opposition, and surprisingly, the US men's Olympic hockey team. The Americast hosts—Justin Webb, Sarah Smith, and Anthony Zurcher—highlight the challenge Trump faces amid declining approval ratings and increasing voter dissatisfaction. The episode evaluates whether his detailed pitch could influence voter sentiment ahead of the upcoming midterm elections. Listeners are encouraged to engage via Discord, WhatsApp, or email, and the podcast is available on BBC Sounds, including smart speakers. Produced

item.title: Modi Visits Israel Amid Gaza War as India Balances Strategic Ties in the Middle East
item.description:
Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi is undertaking a two-day visit to Israel, marking his first trip to the country since the war in Gaza began. During the visit, Modi is scheduled to address Israel's parliament, the Knesset, and hold meetings with Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and President Isaac Herzog. Notably, there are no meetings planned with Palestinian leaders. The trip highlights the deepening relationship between India and Israel, particularly in defence, technology, counter-terrorism and trade. Since Modi first visited Israel in 2017—the first such visit by an Indian prime minister—bilateral ties have expanded significantly, with India becoming one of the largest buyers of Israeli arms.
The visit comes at a delicate time in regional politics. India has condemned the 7 October Hamas-led attacks on Israel while also expressing concern about civilian casualties in Gaza and reaffirming its support for a two-state solution. Analysts suggest that Modi’s trip will test India’s

item.title: Prince Harry and Meghan visit Jordan to support displaced communities
item.description: Prince Harry and Meghan, the Duke and Duchess of Sussex, have arrived in Jordan for a two-day visit focused on humanitarian efforts for communities affected by conflict and displacement. This marks their first international trip together in 18 months. The visit was organized at the invitation of Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, director-general of the World Health Organization (WHO), and includes engagements with WHO officials, Jordanian leaders, and frontline health and mental health programs. The couple is expected to meet staff from World Central Kitchen coordinating food relief for Gaza and visit Questscope's youth center to hear from young participants in creative and wellbeing initiatives. Harry and Meghan have previously partnered with the WHO on vaccine equity campaigns and global initiatives to end violence against children. Since relocating to California in 2020, the couple has undertaken several foreign visits, including trips to Colombia and Nigeria for social and charitable

item.title: NHS Secures Alternative Bone Cement Supplies After Production Fault Disrupts Main Provider
item.description:
The NHS has secured two alternative suppliers of medical bone cement after a production fault at its primary provider, Heraeus Medical, threatened to disrupt planned surgeries across the UK. The issue, caused by a packaging defect identified last week, temporarily halted production and is expected to create a supply disruption lasting at least two months. Bone cement is a critical material used in orthopaedic procedures, particularly hip and knee replacements, where it anchors artificial joints to bone and fills the space between implants and the patient’s natural bone.
Prior to securing alternatives, NHS officials warned that existing stock levels were sufficient for only around two weeks, potentially leaving a supply gap of six to eight weeks. Hospitals were advised to review waiting lists and prioritise patients with the most urgent clinical needs to maximise the limited supplies available. The British Orthopaedic Association also recommended prioritisation to minimise the impact o

item.title: UK Energy Price Cap to Decrease in April Amid Ongoing Supply Risks
item.description: The UK energy price cap, which regulates household electricity and gas bills, is set to decrease by £117 (7%) from April 2026, reaching an average annual level of £1,641. While this is a relief compared to peak post-invasion prices of £4,279 following Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine, bills remain significantly higher than pre-war averages. Although wholesale gas prices have dropped, they continue to fluctuate due to global supply risks. Policy costs, including investments in renewable energy, nuclear power, and grid upgrades, remain embedded in household bills. The government has removed £150 in green levies from energy bills, but these costs are now shifted to general taxation. Looking ahead, the July energy price cap may not fall further due to volatile markets and potential geopolitical tensions, particularly concerns about disruptions to liquefied natural gas (LNG) supplies if conflict escalates with Iran. Analysts warn that Europe’s dependence on alternative sources since losing

item.title: Trump Emphasizes Immigration and Economic Record in State of the Union Address
item.description:
US President Donald Trump delivered a lengthy State of the Union address at the US Capitol, lasting nearly one hour and 48 minutes, in which he highlighted what he described as the major achievements of his administration and outlined priorities for the coming year. According to the BBC’s analysis by Daniel Bush, two dominant themes shaped the speech: immigration and the economy. These issues were presented not only as policy successes but also as strategic focal points aimed at strengthening political support.
On immigration, Trump reiterated his administration’s commitment to border security and stricter enforcement measures. He framed these policies as essential to national safety, economic stability, and the protection of American workers. By emphasizing efforts to curb illegal immigration and reform asylum processes, Trump appealed to voters who view border control as a top priority.
Economic performance formed the second central pillar of the speech. Trump pointed to job growth

item.title: Ex-K-pop CEO Offers $18M Payout to Protect NewJeans from Legal Battles
item.description: Min Hee-jin, former CEO of the K-pop label Ador, has offered to give up a 25.6 billion won ($17.9 million) payout if Hybe, the parent company, drops all lawsuits against the girl group NewJeans. The conflict arose after Hybe terminated member Danielle Marsh's contract and filed claims for contract violations and damages. Following Min's dismissal as CEO in August 2024, NewJeans expressed a desire to leave the label, but a South Korean court ruled last October that they must honor their contracts until 2029. In a recent court decision, Min won a separate dispute involving a put option, granting her the 25.6 billion won payout, which she now offers to forgo for the sake of the group. Min emphasized that her decision prioritizes the well-being of NewJeans members over financial gain and called for all ongoing civil and criminal lawsuits against the group, former employees, and even fans to be withdrawn. Hybe has not yet responded to her offer. NewJeans, who debuted in 2022 and quickly bec

item.title: Paramount Raises Bid for Warner Bros Amid Netflix Competition
item.description: Paramount Skydance has raised its offer to acquire Warner Bros Discovery, increasing its bid by $1 per share to $31 in cash, with additional compensation if completion is delayed. The company, supported by billionaire Larry Ellison and led by his son David Ellison, has been pursuing Warner Bros since last year in an effort to expand its presence in Hollywood. Warner Bros had previously agreed to sell its film and streaming assets, including HBO, to Netflix for $27.75 per share, a deal valued at approximately $82 billion including debt. Paramount’s revised offer also includes a $7 billion breakup fee and covers the $2.8 billion fee Warner Bros would owe Netflix if the original merger were canceled. Warner Bros’ board has not made a final decision, stating it will continue discussions to determine if a superior proposal can be reached. Lawmakers have raised concerns about both offers regarding potential monopolies and the broader impact on the entertainment industry. Netflix co-CEO Ted S

item.title: Lily Allen Announces Relationship with Jonah Freud, Descendant of Sigmund Freud
item.description: Lily Allen has officially confirmed her new relationship with Jonah Freud, the great-great-grandson of the renowned psychoanalyst Sigmund Freud. This announcement comes nearly a year after her separation from actor David Harbour. The couple had previously fueled speculation after being seen together in Paris and Rome, including appearances at Paris Fashion Week. Allen mentioned the relationship casually in a recent Grazia interview, referring to Jonah as “my boyfriend” when asked about her recent messages. The singer’s new romance coincides with a significant career period, following the release of her personal album 'West End Girl,' which was written and recorded in just ten days and reflects on her past marriage breakdown. The album has received critical acclaim, ranking at No. 13 on NME’s 50 Best Albums of 2025 list, and has supported a largely sold-out tour. Notably, her song 'Pussy Palace' became a standout track of the year. Allen is now preparing for a UK theatre and arena tour,

item.title: Women warn that drink spiking can occur in rural areas as well as cities
item.description: Two women from rural Wales have come forward to share their experiences of drink spiking, highlighting that the crime is not limited to large cities. Tirion Richards, 31, from Brechfa, experienced severe symptoms after being spiked in Cardigan, Ceredigion, in January, including being unable to move, see, or lift her head. She was helped by friends and eventually collapsed at her sister's house, later realizing through medical tests that she had been drugged. Another woman, Lorraine Beattie, described being spiked at a birthday gathering in the same town, losing consciousness, and waking up in hospital covered in vomit. Both women stressed the seriousness of the crime and the risks it poses, particularly when victims are alone or without immediate support. Dawn Dines, CEO of the charity Stamp Out Spiking, emphasized that spiking can occur anywhere, not just in big cities, and called for better training for bar staff and security, as well as preventative measures like drink covers. A Hom

item.title: Virginia Governor Abigail Spanberger Criticizes Trump’s Policies in State of the Union Rebuttal
item.description:
Abigail Spanberger, recently elected as Virginia’s first female governor, delivered the Democratic response to President Donald Trump’s State of the Union address, sharply criticizing his economic and immigration policies. In her speech, Spanberger questioned whether the president was genuinely working to make life more affordable and safer for Americans. She structured her rebuttal around three central questions: whether Trump is lowering costs, protecting national security at home and abroad, and working in the interests of ordinary citizens. According to Spanberger, the answer to all three is no.
She condemned the administration’s immigration enforcement actions, particularly Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) raids conducted in Democratic-led cities. Referring to operations in Minneapolis that resulted in the fatal shootings of two US citizens, Alex Pretti and Renee Good, Spanberger accused federal agents of acting without warrants and lacking accountability. Following publ

item.title: UK-Designed Missile Strikes Russian Defense Plant Amid Ukraine Conflict
item.description: On February 25, 2026, Ukraine launched a medium-range missile targeting the Votkinsk Machine-Building Plant in Udmurtia, a key Russian defense facility involved in strategic missile production. The missile, known as the FP-5 Flamingo, caused significant damage to Workshop 19, a hub for producing ICBMs such as Topol-M, Yars, and submarine-launched Bulava missiles, injuring at least 11 workers. The attack has raised concerns about Russia's ability to maintain its strategic missile production and coincides with escalating tensions over NATO and U.S. missile deployments in Ukraine. The FP-5 Flamingo, designed by the UK with some Ukrainian components, represents a new capability for Ukraine to strike deep into Russian territory. Russian officials have viewed such deployments as a direct threat to national security, and previous warnings and failed negotiations with the U.S. and NATO have heightened the stakes. Analysts warn that the attack could be interpreted as a provocation by the West,

item.title: Key Themes and Political Divisions Highlighted in Trump’s State of the Union Address
item.description:
US President Donald Trump delivered his State of the Union address to a joint session of Congress, outlining his administration’s achievements over the past year and presenting his legislative priorities for the months ahead. In the speech, Trump emphasized foreign policy challenges, including tensions with Iran, and underscored his administration’s stance on national security and international negotiations. He also touched on America’s global standing, referencing the Olympic Games as a symbol of national pride and unity.
The address reflected deep partisan divisions within Congress. While Republican lawmakers frequently applauded the president’s remarks, many Democrats responded with visible skepticism, restrained reactions, and at times open disagreement. These contrasting responses illustrated the broader political polarization shaping Washington, particularly as the country approaches another election cycle.
Trump highlighted economic performance, job growth, and border security

item.title: Russia Increases Oil Exports Despite Falling Revenues
item.description: Despite ongoing Western sanctions and efforts to block its oil trade since the beginning of the Ukraine War four years ago, Russia's crude oil exports have not diminished. According to a report by the Finnish Centre for Research on Energy and Clean Air (CREA), Russia exported 215 million tons of crude in the fourth year of the war, marking a 6% decrease in volume compared to the previous year but a 6% increase over pre-war levels. However, revenue from these exports fell 18% in one year, showing that Moscow had to sell oil at a discount. Total fossil fuel revenues reached €193 billion, down 19% from the previous year and 27% below pre-war levels, yet still substantial. China, India, and Turkey remained Russia's main buyers, accounting for 93% of crude exports, although their annual imports slightly declined due to U.S. sanctions. European buyers like Hungary and Slovakia continue to rely on Russian oil under EU exemptions, with Hungary increasing its dependence to 92%. Russia also miti

item.title: European Military Aid to Ukraine Reaches Its Limits Amid Shortages
item.description: European countries are reportedly reaching the limits of their military support to Ukraine, as key nations indicate they can no longer supply additional arms. Finnish Foreign Minister Elina Valtonen stated that Finland has already sent all available assistance to Kyiv, despite its contributions being above the European average. Germany's Defense Minister Boris Pistorius confirmed that German arsenals are exhausted, while Denmark emphasized its focus on financing drone production rather than providing more equipment. This shortage comes at a critical time for Ukraine, which faces severe ammunition shortages for its air defense systems. Meanwhile, Russian missile attacks continue to target Ukrainian energy infrastructure with minimal resistance. The European hope now rests on a €90 billion aid package, which is stalled due to Hungary’s refusal to approve it. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has urged European partners to respond to his military’s needs, but repeated statements of d

item.title: Two Arrested After Sydney Grandfather Allegedly Kidnapped and Killed in Case of Mistaken Identity
item.description:
Australian authorities have arrested two men in connection with the alleged kidnapping and murder of 85-year-old Sydney grandfather Chris Baghsarian, who police believe was taken in a case of mistaken identity. Baghsarian was abducted from his home in North Ryde during a pre-dawn raid on 13 February. Investigators have stated they are 'a million percent confident' that he was not the intended target and that neither he nor his family had any links to organised crime. Local media reports suggest the perpetrators may have been targeting another family believed to have criminal associations.
Human remains discovered near a golf course on Sydney’s outskirts were formally identified as Baghsarian following forensic testing. The arrests of two men, aged 24 and 29, came shortly after the identification. Police indicated that both suspects were previously known to authorities for minor offences and are expected to be formally charged. Investigators also believe additional individuals were inv

item.title: Trump Denies Reports of Top General's Caution on Iran Strikes, Claims Any Action Would Be 'Easily Won'
item.description: President Donald Trump has publicly rejected media reports suggesting that General Dan Caine, Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, advised caution regarding potential US air strikes on Iran. According to outlets like Axios and The Washington Post, Caine and other Pentagon officials expressed concerns in internal discussions that military action could escalate into a prolonged regional conflict, involving retaliatory attacks from Iranian proxies, significant risks to US forces, and the need for a larger troop commitment. These warnings come amid a substantial US military build-up in the Middle East, including the deployment of the USS Gerald R. Ford aircraft carrier and additional naval assets, aimed at pressuring Iran over its nuclear program. Trump, posting on Truth Social, labeled the reports 'fake news' and asserted that Caine, while preferring to avoid war, believes any military engagement with Iran would be decisively victorious and quickly concluded. He emphasized Caine's focus

item.title: Senegal's Prime Minister Introduces Bill to Increase Prison Sentences for Same-Sex Relations to Up to 10 Years
item.description: Senegal's Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko has submitted a draft bill to parliament that would significantly strengthen existing laws criminalizing same-sex relations in the conservative, Muslim-majority West African nation. The proposed legislation, approved by the cabinet last week and presented to lawmakers on Tuesday, seeks to double the maximum prison term for 'acts against nature'—defined as any sexual conduct between two people of the same sex—from five years to ten years, with sentences ranging from five to ten years. If the act involves a minor, the maximum penalty would apply. The bill also introduces prison terms of three to seven years for promoting or advocating same-sex relations, along with fines up to 10 million CFA francs (approximately $18,000 or £13,000). It further prescribes punishments for unfounded accusations of homosexuality. Sonko emphasized that the offense would remain a misdemeanor rather than being elevated to a more serious crime category. This move follows a

item.title: After Cartel Rampage, Puerto Vallarta Works to Restore Calm Amid Visible Damage
item.description:
The BBC reports from Puerto Vallarta, a popular coastal resort town in Mexico’s Jalisco state, following a wave of cartel-related violence that left visible destruction across parts of the city. Journalist Will Grant visited areas affected by Sunday’s unrest, where evidence of the rampage remains clear. In a Costco car park, several vehicles were left burned and damaged, serving as stark reminders of the sudden outbreak of violence that disrupted daily life and tourism in the area.
The violence formed part of a broader pattern of cartel activity that has affected multiple regions in Mexico. In Puerto Vallarta, a destination known for attracting both domestic and international tourists, the events caused fear and uncertainty. Some visitors found themselves temporarily trapped as security concerns and transport disruptions unfolded. As calm gradually returns, tourists are now making their way to the city’s international airport, resuming travel plans that had been interrupted.
Local au

item.title: Brandi Carlile Raises $600,000 for Families Affected by Immigration Enforcement in Minneapolis
item.description: Grammy-winning singer Brandi Carlile transformed her Minneapolis concert into a significant fundraiser, generating over $600,000 to support families impacted by recent Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) actions in the city. The event, titled 'Be Human: A Concert for Minneapolis,' was held at the Target Center on February 21 and livestreamed for broader access, drawing more than 12,000 attendees. Proceeds were donated to the Minnesota-based organization Advocates for Human Rights, which works to support and represent families affected by immigration enforcement. Carlile also raised additional funds through the sale of $40 'Be Human' T-shirts, inspired by her latest album 'Human.' The concert responded to Operation Metro Surge, a federal initiative deploying up to 3,000 ICE and Border Patrol agents to Minneapolis and Saint Paul. Reports indicate widespread detentions, including U.S. citizens, which led to public outcry and a city-wide strike. During the show, Carlile performed wit

item.title: Former Nigerian Oil Minister Faces Court Over Alleged Bribery from Oil Executives
item.description: Diezani Alison-Madueke, former Nigerian Minister of Petroleum Resources, is facing trial in the UK over allegations of accepting bribes from two Nigerian oil tycoons, Olajide Omokore and Kolawole Aluko, during her tenure between 2010 and 2015. UK prosecutors claim Alison-Madueke enjoyed a lavish lifestyle in the UK funded by these businessmen seeking oil contracts. Court evidence includes recordings from her seized Samsung phone, capturing her confronting Omokore and Aluko about their actions, which she believed could compromise her. In the conversations, Alison-Madueke expresses frustration over perceived blackmail and warns of revealing the full details publicly. She denies five counts of accepting bribes and a charge of conspiracy to commit bribery. The trial also involves Olatimbo Ayinde, accused of bribery relating to Alison-Madueke, and her brother, Doye Agama, charged with conspiracy. The defense argues that Alison-Madueke did not solicit or receive financial advantages and that

item.title: Bomb threat against Australian Prime Minister linked to Chinese dance group's tour
item.description: A bomb threat directed at Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese originated from emails connected to the Shen Yun Chinese dance and music group, whose performances are banned in China. The threats demanded that Shen Yun cancel its upcoming shows in Australia, warning that explosives had been placed around the Prime Minister's official residence, known as the Lodge, in Canberra. Authorities confirmed attending an 'alleged security incident' but found nothing suspicious. Albanese was evacuated from his home as a precaution and taken to a secure location. One of the emails, written in Chinese, warned that the Lodge would be 'blown into ruins' and that 'blood will flow like a river' if the performances continued. The news was first reported by a newspaper associated with the Falun Gong movement, which is also banned in China and has connections with Shen Yun. Shen Yun reported the threat to the police. The Australian Federal Police declined to comment publicly on the emails. Albanese l

item.title: Key Highlights and Political Tensions in Trump’s 2026 State of the Union Address
item.description:
President Donald Trump delivered his 2026 State of the Union address in what became the longest speech of its kind, presenting a forceful defense of his administration’s policies while confronting a sharply divided Congress. Speaking at a pivotal political moment, Trump addressed ongoing tensions with Iran, economic concerns over affordability in the United States, and the recent Supreme Court decision that struck down his global tariff policy.
A notable moment occurred as Trump encountered members of the Supreme Court for the first time since criticizing their ruling against his tariffs. Only four of the nine justices attended. The interaction with Chief Justice John Roberts appeared tense, reflecting lingering friction. Trump reiterated his disappointment with the court’s decision and announced a new 15% tariff under Section 122, a rarely used law that allows temporary tariffs for 150 days without immediate congressional approval. He suggested congressional action would not be neces

item.title: Geometric Projection Mapping in Natural Landscapes by Javier Riera
item.description: Spanish designer Javier Riera creates striking geometric projections that interact with natural landscapes, transforming organic environments into visual experiments in shape and light. Using custom-mapped projections, Riera overlays rigid geometric forms onto forests, deserts, and other outdoor settings, creating a dialogue between structured design and natural randomness. This interplay highlights contrasts between human-made order and the organic irregularity of nature, inviting viewers to reconsider their perceptions of space and form. His work is not only visually arresting but also conceptually engaging, as it explores the relationship between architecture, art, and the environment. Riera's approach combines precision with creativity, resulting in images that appear both surreal and harmonious. By projecting geometric patterns onto trees, rocks, and landscapes, he creates temporary installations that merge technology with the natural world, emphasizing the potential of projection

item.title: Essential Maintenance Tips to Preserve Your Property’s Appearance
item.description: Maintaining the visual appeal of a property requires proactive care and understanding the factors that cause damage over time. Environmental elements like pollutants, mineral deposits, algae, and UV rays gradually deteriorate surfaces, often unnoticed until damage becomes visible. Regular cleaning restores the original colors of exterior walls, concrete, and stucco, with methods like hot water pressure washing effectively removing deep-set grime. Timing is crucial: spring cleaning addresses winter salt and mold, while fall cleaning prepares surfaces for freezing temperatures. Different materials need tailored approaches; wood, stone, and concrete require specific pressures and chemicals to avoid permanent damage. Windows benefit from professional cleaning to remove hard water spots, rust, pollen, and city pollution, improving sunlight transmission and even the efficiency of solar panels. Protective coatings such as sealants, epoxy, and specialized metal treatments prevent moisture, sun

item.title: Thailand's Progressive Party Loses Ground to Established Political Families in Provincial Elections
item.description: In Thailand, the progressive People's Party (formerly Move Forward) suffered significant losses in the 2026 elections, particularly in provincial areas dominated by entrenched political families. Chutiphong Pipoppinyo, a young former MP from Rayong, described the challenges of competing against the Pitutecha family, who have long controlled local politics through a system known as 'Baan Yai.' This patronage network relies on social connections, canvassers, and financial incentives, making it difficult for new candidates with limited resources to gain influence. Despite the People's Party's strong performance in urban centers like Bangkok and Chiang Mai, they lost over 20% of their seats nationwide, with the conservative Bhumjaithai party dramatically increasing its representation. The elections highlighted the divide between urban voters, receptive to reformist ideas, and rural communities that still rely on traditional patronage networks for access to resources. Political analysts not

item.title: Trump emphasizes achievements and policy priorities in State of the Union address
item.description: Donald Trump delivered a combative State of the Union address, framing the United States as experiencing a historic economic and social turnaround. He highlighted rising incomes, a growing stock market, lower petrol prices, reduced undocumented migration at the southern border, and tamed inflation as evidence that 'the country is winning again.' Despite these claims, public approval for Trump remains around 40%, and the address offered few new policies, mostly reiterating past proposals such as healthcare payments, voter ID requirements, tariffs, and stricter immigration enforcement. Trump's discussion of tariffs caused unease among some Republicans, while his focus on immigration drew enthusiastic applause from supporters and criticism from Democrats. The speech largely avoided foreign policy, even amid heightened tensions near Iran. Observers noted that Trump appeared to be appealing to patriotic sentiment and his base in anticipation of upcoming midterm elections. The address includ

item.title: DNA-Inspired Molecular System Advances Solar Thermal Energy Storage
item.description:
Researchers from the University of California, Santa Barbara and UCLA have developed a new molecular solar thermal (MOST) energy storage system inspired by DNA photochemistry. The system is based on a modified 2-pyrimidone molecule, chemically related to thymine, one of the bases in DNA. Drawing from the mechanism behind UV-induced DNA damage, the researchers engineered a molecule that absorbs ultraviolet light and rearranges into a high-energy “Dewar” isomer. This strained molecular configuration acts as a rechargeable thermal battery, storing solar energy in chemical bonds and releasing it later as heat.
The new compound achieves an energy density of 1.65 megajoules per kilogram (MJ/kg), significantly outperforming previous MOST candidates such as norbornadiene (0.97 MJ/kg) and azaborinine (0.65 MJ/kg), and approaching levels competitive with lithium-ion batteries for thermal storage. Unlike fossil fuels, which are single-use chemical energy stores, this system is rechargeable and c

item.title: Nicola Sturgeon Claims Alex Salmond Sought Retaliation Over Harassment Complaints
item.description: Former First Minister Nicola Sturgeon accused her predecessor Alex Salmond of pursuing a 'revenge mission' after the Scottish government's handling of harassment complaints against him. In a February 2021 interview with James Hamilton, her independent adviser on the ministerial code, Sturgeon suggested that Salmond stirred a 'hornet's nest' by contacting government officials regarding allegations of inappropriate behavior at Edinburgh Airport in 2017. Salmond successfully sued the government in 2019 for mishandling these complaints. Salmond later claimed Sturgeon made false statements about the investigation and that a leak to the Daily Record could only have originated from her office or advisers. Sturgeon denied knowledge of any leaks in her memoir. The Scottish government has faced legal action for failing to release documents related to Hamilton's inquiry on time, with the Information Commissioner initiating proceedings. The row over document publication began after a freedom of in

item.title: Hong Kong Protests Panama’s Takeover of Canal Ports Amid Legal and Geopolitical Dispute
item.description:
Hong Kong has formally lodged a strong protest against Panama after the Panamanian government took control of two key container ports located along the Panama Canal. The ports had been operated for more than 20 years by CK Hutchison, a Hong Kong-based conglomerate founded by billionaire Li Ka-shing. The dispute began after Panama’s Supreme Court annulled the contracts that granted the company the right to manage the ports, ruling that the agreements were unconstitutional. In response, Hong Kong authorities accused Panama of forcibly taking over the facilities and undermining both contractual obligations and international trade norms.
The decision comes amid heightened geopolitical tensions surrounding the strategic waterway. Former US President Donald Trump has repeatedly claimed that China operates or controls the Panama Canal, assertions for which there is no public evidence. Despite these claims, the canal is administered by the Panama Canal Authority, a government agency of Panama

item.title: Japan Sets 2030 Timeline for Surface-to-Air Missile Deployment on Yonaguni Island Amid China Tensions
item.description:
Japan has announced plans to deploy medium-range surface-to-air missiles on its westernmost island, Yonaguni, by fiscal year 2030, which ends in March 2031. Defence Minister Shinjiro Koizumi confirmed the timeline, marking the first time Tokyo has specified when the missile unit will become operational since the plan was first revealed in 2022. Yonaguni lies just 110km from Taiwan and is visible from its shores on clear days, making it strategically significant as tensions between China and Taiwan continue to rise.
China claims Taiwan as its territory and has not ruled out using force to achieve reunification. Concerns have grown that a conflict over Taiwan could draw in the United States and regional allies such as Japan. Tensions between Tokyo and Beijing have worsened in recent months, particularly after Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi suggested Japan could activate its self-defence forces in the event of an attack on Taiwan. Beijing has responded with measures including export restr

item.title: Kyren Wilson seals Welsh Open victory with decisive trick shot on final black
item.description:
Kyren Wilson produced a moment of brilliance during his match against Liu Hongyu at the Welsh Open, finishing in style with an audacious trick shot on the final black to secure victory and reach a total break of 142. The impressive shot not only demonstrated Wilson’s technical ability but also his confidence under pressure, as it came at a crucial stage of the frame. The final black is often a routine pot for professionals, but Wilson chose a more flamboyant approach, adding flair to an already commanding performance.
The match formed part of the Welsh Open, one of the key tournaments on the professional snooker calendar. Wilson’s display highlighted why he remains one of the sport’s top competitors, capable of combining tactical intelligence with creative shot-making. His 142 total showcased strong break-building throughout the frame, reflecting consistency and focus against a determined opponent in Liu Hongyu.
The BBC Sport clip captures the decisive moment, offering fans the chanc

item.title: Serie A Faces Possible Absence from Champions League Last 16 After Inter Exit
item.description:
Italian football is facing the possibility of having no representatives in the Champions League last 16 for the first time in nearly four decades. Since 1987-88, at least one Italian club has reached the knockout stages of Europe’s top competition. However, Inter Milan’s unexpected play-off elimination by Norwegian side Bodo/Glimt has placed that record in serious jeopardy. Inter, last season’s finalists and current Serie A leaders, were defeated 3-1 in the first leg and 2-1 at San Siro, marking the first time they have been knocked out by a club from outside Europe’s top five leagues.
Juventus and Atalanta must now overturn significant first-leg deficits to preserve Italy’s presence in the tournament. Juventus trail Galatasaray 5-2 ahead of their home leg in Turin, while Atalanta are 2-0 down against Borussia Dortmund. Failure by both clubs would result in no Italian teams reaching the last 16, a scenario described by journalists as a 'debacle' and symbolic of broader structural issu

item.title: Newcastle confident ahead of Champions League last-16 draw after strong European run
item.description:
Newcastle United have sent a clear message of confidence to potential Champions League opponents after securing their place in the last 16 with a 3-2 win over Qarabag, completing a dominant 9-3 aggregate victory. Winger Jacob Murphy expressed the squad’s fearless mentality, stating they are ready to face any opponent, including European giants Barcelona or Chelsea. Manager Eddie Howe echoed that sentiment, insisting his team must believe they can raise their level and compete with anyone in knockout football.
The Magpies have enjoyed an impressive European campaign, scoring 26 goals and winning six matches — their best return in a single Champions League season. This resurgence comes after a difficult spell in the Premier League, including a home defeat to Brentford that prompted self-reflection from Howe and a renewed commitment from the players. Since then, Newcastle have won four of their last five matches in all competitions, demonstrating resilience and unity.
Notable results in

item.title: Bodo/Glimt Reach Champions League Last 16 After Historic Aggregate Win Over Inter Milan
item.description:
Bodo/Glimt produced one of the most remarkable results in recent Champions League history by eliminating Inter Milan with a 5-2 aggregate victory to reach the last 16 for the first time. Holding a 3-1 advantage from the first leg, the Norwegian side travelled to the San Siro and delivered a disciplined and determined performance to secure a 2-1 win on the night. Despite facing intense early pressure from the Italian champions, Bodo/Glimt remained composed, repelling repeated attacks before striking decisively.
Jens Petter Hauge once again proved instrumental, scoring his sixth goal of the tournament to open the scoring and later assisting Hakon Evjen for the second. The result completed a stunning home-and-away double over Inter and added to an already impressive European campaign in which the Norwegian club defeated Manchester City and Atletico Madrid during the group phase.
This achievement marks a historic milestone for both the club and Norwegian football. Bodo/Glimt are the firs

item.title: Merz Visits China Amid Growing Concerns Over Germany’s Expanding Trade Deficit
item.description:
German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has arrived in Beijing for his first official visit to China, accompanied by a significant business delegation, as Germany faces mounting concerns over a widening trade imbalance with its largest trading partner. In 2025, imports from China into Germany reached €170.6 billion, an 8.8% annual increase, while German exports to China fell by 9.7% to €81.3 billion. This growing deficit has raised alarm among German industry leaders, particularly in key sectors such as automotive, machinery, and chemicals.
Merz stated before departure that Germany seeks a balanced, reliable, regulated, and fair partnership with China. However, analysts warn that Chinese state subsidies and currency practices may be distorting competition, contributing to what some are calling a new 'China shock' affecting not only Germany but the broader European Union. The issue is compounded by Europe’s rising production costs following the Covid-19 pandemic and Russia’s invasion of Ukrai

item.title: Senator Calls for Investigation into FBI Director’s Private Jet Use Amid Claims of Delayed Responses
item.description:
A senior Democratic senator has called for an investigation into FBI Director Kash Patel over allegations that his personal use of the bureau’s private aircraft delayed responses to major criminal cases. Senator Dick Durbin, the top Democrat on the Senate Judiciary Committee, cited a whistleblower complaint claiming that Patel’s travel arrangements contributed to operational setbacks. According to Durbin, the FBI’s shooting reconstruction team was delayed by at least a day in responding to the killing of conservative commentator Charlie Kirk at a college campus in Utah due to a pilot shortage allegedly linked to Patel’s travel. The same team was also reportedly delayed in responding to a mass shooting at Brown University in Rhode Island, where two students were killed and nine others injured. Instead of flying, the team drove from Quantico, Virginia, to Providence.
The FBI has strongly denied the allegations. Spokesman Ben Williamson described the claims as false and misleading, stati

item.title: Drone Warfare Intensifies in Sudan’s Kordofan Region as Civilian Toll Mounts
item.description:
Sudan’s civil war has entered a new and deadly phase as intensified drone attacks reshape the battlefield in the south-central Kordofan region. Nearly three years after fighting erupted in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF), the epicentre of the conflict has shifted from Khartoum and Darfur to Kordofan, a strategic area rich in oil and gold. Both sides have consolidated gains elsewhere, making Kordofan the pivotal front line.
Near-daily drone strikes have targeted markets, health facilities, residential neighbourhoods and aid convoys, causing significant civilian casualties. The United Nations has condemned the attacks, urging all parties to stop targeting civilian infrastructure. Reports indicate that more than 50 civilians were killed in separate strikes over two days in North and West Kordofan. While local monitors have blamed some attacks on the military, both the SAF and RSF face accusations of indiscriminate strikes

item.title: Government Proposes Changes to SEND System in England Amid Parental Concerns
item.description: The UK government has released its Schools White Paper, outlining major reforms to the Special Educational Needs and Disabilities (SEND) system in England. Starting in September 2029, children's eligibility for Education, Health and Care Plans (EHCPs) will be reassessed at key stages, and by 2035 EHCPs will be limited to children with the most complex needs. New Individual Support Plans (ISPs) will be introduced and categorized as targeted, targeted plus, or specialist. The government plans to invest £4 billion in SEND support over the next three years. While Education Secretary Bridget Phillipson emphasizes the government's commitment, parents and advocates express concerns that the reforms could reduce children's rights and support. Families worry that children who have benefited from EHCPs may lose essential support during secondary school. Advocates like Marsha Martin and Ailith Harley-Roberts highlight risks of destabilizing children, particularly for neurodivergent students. Educ

item.title: UK MPs Critique Prince Andrew but Show Limited Appetite for Monarchy Reform
item.description: In a recent debate in the UK House of Commons, MPs expressed unusually blunt criticism of Prince Andrew, calling him 'rude, arrogant, and entitled,' reflecting the near-total collapse of respect for him among both the Royal Family and politicians. The discussion was prompted by a Liberal Democrat request for the release of documents related to Andrew's 25-year-old appointment as a trade envoy. While the government has agreed to publish these documents, other demands for broader scrutiny of the monarchy remain cautious. The debate highlighted that long-standing parliamentary conventions have often shielded the royal family from criticism, but the disfavor surrounding Andrew appears to have created an exception. Despite the high-profile nature of the revelations and the former prince's arrest, MPs did not use the debate to argue for the abolition of the monarchy or for fundamental constitutional change. While there are parliamentarians who favor an elected head of state, such views remai

item.title: US avoids commemorating war anniversary; 'Prince of Darkness' comment noted
item.description: The BBC article mentions that the United States has chosen not to observe a specific war anniversary, reflecting current political or societal stances regarding historical military events. Additionally, the phrase 'Prince of Darkness' appears, likely referring to a notable figure or comment reported recently. While the article provides minimal details, the focus seems to be on diplomatic or cultural choices surrounding remembrance and public discourse on historical and potentially controversial topics. The report may also summarize reactions from various media outlets and public perspectives, as several related publications are referenced. Given the limited information available in the excerpt, the article seems to touch on international relations, historical memory, and media commentary, highlighting how certain events are acknowledged—or ignored—by prominent nations.

item.title: Welsh Parliament Approves Consent for Assisted Dying Legislation
item.description: The Senedd in Wales has approved a legislative consent motion that allows the implementation of assisted dying services within the NHS, pending final approval from Westminster. The vote followed a highly emotional debate, with 28 members in favor, 23 against, and two abstentions. While the motion permits Wales to regulate assisted dying services, it does not determine whether assisted dying becomes legal, as this depends on the progress of the bill in the House of Lords. Proponents argued that rejecting the motion would leave Wales reliant on private providers, restricting access to those who can afford it. Critics raised concerns about moral implications, potential risks for vulnerable groups, and the impact on palliative care funding. Labour and Plaid Cymru members were split in their votes, with senior ministers opposing, while all Conservative members voted against. The legislation’s final approval would leave the next Welsh government responsible for establishing regulations. Supp

item.title: UK Energy Bills Set to Decrease in April Following Government Policy Changes
item.description: Energy bills in England, Wales, and Scotland are set to decrease in April due to government changes in policy costs. The upcoming reduction, expected to be announced by Ofgem, will affect nearly all households regardless of their energy tariff, though the exact savings will vary depending on household size, energy usage, and type of fuel. Analysts at Cornwall Insight forecast an average household saving of around £117, lowering typical bills to £1,641 annually. The reduction will primarily be applied through lower electricity unit costs, meaning high electricity users, including vulnerable households with medical equipment, will benefit most. Fixed tariff customers will also see reductions as suppliers communicate new rates in the coming weeks. Despite this decrease, energy prices remain historically high, and households are encouraged to explore competitive fixed deals to maximize savings. Other household costs, including water bills, council tax, and general living expenses, are risi

item.title: How Age-Verification Laws Are Expanding Digital Surveillance and Undermining Privacy
item.description:
The article examines the growing push for mandatory age-verification systems on social media platforms and argues that these measures create a structural conflict with data-privacy principles. As lawmakers seek to protect minors from harmful online content, they increasingly require platforms to take “reasonable” or “effective” steps to prevent underage access. However, the author contends that meaningful enforcement inevitably requires collecting and retaining personal data, which contradicts modern privacy standards that emphasize data minimization and limited retention.
Platforms typically rely on two main approaches: identity-based verification, such as uploading government IDs, and inference-based systems, including facial age estimation and behavioral analysis. In practice, companies combine both methods. Self-declared ages are monitored by AI systems, and when uncertainty arises, users may be asked for selfies, official documents, or even credit card verification. These process

item.title: Paramount Skydance Submits Final $31-Per-Share Bid for Warner Bros Discovery Amid Competing Netflix Offer
item.description:
Paramount Skydance has submitted a revised “best and final” offer to acquire Warner Bros Discovery (WBD), intensifying a high-stakes takeover battle with Netflix. The new proposal values WBD shares at $31 each, an increase from the previous $30 offer, and includes additional fees. Warner Bros Discovery acknowledged that the improved bid could reasonably lead to a superior proposal compared with existing agreements.
Netflix, which has already signed an agreement with WBD and has been considered the preferred bidder, recently raised its own offer to $27.75 per share in cash. However, the two bids differ in scope. Netflix is seeking to acquire WBD’s production and streaming assets, while Paramount Skydance aims to purchase the entire company. The WBD board granted Paramount Skydance a one-week extension to submit its final offer, with Netflix’s consent. Netflix now has four days to either revise its proposal or withdraw.
Paramount Skydance’s pursuit has included a hostile takeover attem

item.title: Richard Engel Describes Devastation and Limited Access in Post-Ceasefire Gaza During IDF-Guided Visit
item.description: Four months after a ceasefire agreement ended intense fighting between Israel and Hamas in Gaza, Sky News correspondent Richard Engel provides an update on the situation in the enclave.
