item.title: FBI Investigates Prior Visit by Suspect Before Nancy Guthrie’s Disappearance
item.description: Authorities investigating the disappearance of Nancy Guthrie, mother of NBC News anchor Savannah Guthrie, say that the prime suspect may have been at her home prior to the night she went missing. The 84-year-old was reported missing on February 1 in Tucson, Arizona, and police suspect she was abducted. The FBI has released doorbell camera footage showing a masked man carrying a backpack, identified as the prime suspect, and separate images showing a masked individual without a backpack captured at Guthrie’s front door. It is unclear when these images were taken, and local authorities emphasize that any assumption they were from different days is speculative. The Pima County Sheriff's Department stated the investigation continues based on verifiable evidence. Authorities believe Guthrie was the target of a planned kidnapping. The family has made public appeals for her safe return and for proof she is alive. The FBI described the suspect as a male approximately 5ft 9in to 5ft 10in (175–1

item.title: UK Regulator Fines Reddit £14.47m for Inadequate Child Age Verification Measures
item.description:
Reddit has been fined £14.47 million by the UK’s Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO) for unlawfully processing children’s personal data and failing to implement effective age verification measures. The regulator concluded that the platform did not properly assess or verify the ages of its users, which increased the risk of children being exposed to inappropriate or harmful content. According to the ICO, Reddit relied primarily on users self-declaring their age when creating an account, a method the authority described as easy to bypass and insufficient under UK data protection standards.
The fine follows regulatory scrutiny under the UK’s Online Safety Act (OSA), which requires online services likely to be accessed by children to introduce stronger safeguards. Although Reddit began implementing age verification checks in July 2025 to align with the new legal framework, the ICO determined that earlier practices failed to meet the required standards for protecting children’s data.

item.title: Scottish Government Rejects Flamingo Land Resort Proposal at Loch Lomond
item.description: The Scottish government has officially rejected plans to build a £40 million Flamingo Land holiday resort on the shores of Loch Lomond. The proposal included a waterpark, monorail, hotel, and restaurants, and had been a source of prolonged controversy between the theme park operator and local residents. Initial approval by the government followed a reversal of the Loch Lomond and the Trossachs Park Authority's rejection in September 2024, but the plans were later recalled due to their environmental and social impacts. Key reasons cited for the rejection include flood risks, loss of historic woodland, and conflicts with broader development policies. Although the government's appointed reporter recommended approval, ministers concluded that the potential damage to the natural and cultural heritage outweighed economic benefits. Scottish Greens co-leader Ross Greer praised the decision, calling it a major victory for the local community and urging Flamingo Land to abandon the project. The

item.title: Thirty Swans Found Dead in East London as Authorities Investigate Possible Avian Flu
item.description:
Health and environmental authorities are investigating the deaths of 30 swans found in the River Thames over the course of a week, raising concerns about a possible new outbreak of avian flu in the United Kingdom. The swans were recovered from waterways in and around West India and Millwall Dock, near Canary Wharf in east London. The Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs (DEFRA) has collected the birds and is conducting tests to determine the exact cause of death.
Ben Macmillan, an ecologist with the Canal & River Trust, confirmed that a specialist contractor retrieved the swans from the affected areas. While the outbreak is currently described as 'suspected,' officials are taking precautionary measures to limit any potential spread of the virus. Members of the public have been strongly advised not to touch dead or visibly sick birds and to report any such findings to DEFRA.
According to the NHS, avian flu primarily affects birds but can, in rare cases, spread to humans th

item.title: Trump Faces Declining Approval and Policy Challenges Ahead of Key State of the Union Address
item.description:
President Donald Trump is set to deliver a pivotal State of the Union address at a critical juncture in his second term. Over the past year, he has aggressively pursued a sweeping agenda, implementing strict immigration enforcement measures, reshaping foreign alliances, expanding executive authority, and imposing controversial trade tariffs. While he has secured some policy victories, many of his actions have proven divisive and, in some cases, unpopular with the broader public.
Recent opinion polls indicate declining approval ratings, with surveys from CNN and The Washington Post showing support below 40%. With midterm elections approaching in eight months, the speech represents a major opportunity for Trump to rally public support and strengthen the Republican position in Congress. A loss of congressional control could result in legislative gridlock and renewed impeachment efforts.
Immigration and economic policy, traditionally areas of strength for Trump, now present political vul

item.title: UK Regulator Fines Reddit £14m for Failing to Implement Effective Child Age Verification Measures
item.description:
Reddit has been fined more than £14 million by the UK’s Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO) following an investigation into the platform’s handling of children’s personal data. The data protection watchdog concluded that the US-based social media company failed to implement effective age verification systems, thereby unlawfully processing the personal information of children under the age of 13.
According to the ICO, Reddit did not apply robust age assurance mechanisms, meaning it could not reliably determine whether users were underage. As a result, the company lacked a lawful basis for collecting and processing children’s data. The investigation also found that Reddit failed to conduct a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) before January 2025 to evaluate and mitigate potential risks to children using the platform.
UK Information Commissioner John Edwards criticized the company, stating that children under 13 had their data collected and used in ways they could not understa

item.title: Samie Elishi and Ciaran Davies Win Love Island: All Stars Series After Six Weeks of Competition
item.description: The winners of Love Island: All Stars have been announced after six weeks of drama, challenges, and recoupling. Samie Elishi and Ciaran Davies emerged as the winners, securing the £50,000 prize following a public vote. Millie Court and Zac Woodworth came in second, while Scott van-der-Sluis and Leanne Amaning finished third. The series faced delays due to South African wildfires and the introduction of six American contestants in a separate villa, only two of whom reached the final. Samie and Ciaran, who had been exclusive during their time in the villa, expressed surprise and excitement at their win. Their post-show plans include a weekend in Barry Island, Wales. The series drew significant streaming numbers, with the final watched by around 659,000 viewers on TV and over 100 million streams across ITVX and YouTube. Love Island continues to maintain popularity, especially on digital platforms, and ITV confirmed that the main series will return for its 13th season in Mallorca this summ

item.title: BBC apologizes for airing racial slur during Bafta Film Awards; questions raised over speech edits
item.description: Labour MP Dawn Butler has requested an urgent explanation from the BBC following the broadcast of a racial slur during the Bafta Film Awards. The incident involved John Davidson, a guest with Tourette syndrome and inspiration behind the film 'I Swear,' who shouted the slur while two Black actors, Delroy Lindo and Michael B. Jordan, were on stage. The BBC acknowledged the mistake, stating the slur was not edited out before the delayed broadcast and has since been removed from iPlayer. Butler criticized the BBC for failing to prevent the airing, noting the program was shown two hours after the live event and describing the slur's broadcast as 'painful and unforgivable.' She also questioned why parts of Akinola Davies Jr.'s acceptance speech, including references to Palestine and migrants' stories, were edited out, while the offensive language remained. Several figures, including Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch and Bafta emerging talent judge Jonte Richardson, condemned the handling of

item.title: Russell Brand Denies New Rape and Sexual Assault Allegations in London Court
item.description: British comedian and actor Russell Brand, 50, appeared at Southwark Crown Court in London on February 24, 2026, to plead not guilty to two new charges of rape and sexual assault. The alleged incidents reportedly occurred in 2009, involving two women. This development follows earlier charges filed last year, in which Brand faced two counts of rape, one count of indecent assault, and two counts of sexual assault against four women between 1999 and 2005. Brand had previously pleaded not guilty to those charges in May 2023, with the trial scheduled for June 2024. A court hearing next month will determine whether the new accusations will be included in the same trial. Brand’s lawyer has requested additional time to respond to the latest allegations. Known for his flamboyant style, outspoken personality, and past fame as a radio presenter for the BBC, television host, and actor in films such as Get Him to the Greek (2010), Brand arrived at court wearing a white cowboy hat and sunglasses. Whe

item.title: France Restricts US Ambassador Charles Kushner's Access to Government Officials After He Skips Summoned Meeting
item.description: Tensions between France and the United States have escalated following a diplomatic incident involving US Ambassador to France Charles Kushner, the father of Jared Kushner and a controversial appointee pardoned by Donald Trump in 2020 for tax evasion and illegal campaign contributions. The row centers on comments posted by the US State Department's counterterrorism bureau and reposted by the US Embassy in Paris regarding the death of 23-year-old Quentin Deranque, a far-right activist and mathematics student who died from head injuries sustained in a violent clash in Lyon on February 12, 2026. The US statement described Deranque as killed by left-wing militants, highlighted the rise of violent radical leftism, and expressed concern over threats to public safety. French officials, including Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot, rejected what they called foreign interference in national affairs and attempts to politicize the incident. Barrot summoned Kushner to explain the comments, but the

item.title: University of Southampton Student Union to Make Plant-Based Meals the Default from 2026
item.description:
The University of Southampton has announced that plant-based meals will become the default option at its student union catering facilities starting in the 2026/27 academic year, following a vote by its student council on 19 February. Under the new system, vegetarian and vegan dishes will be the standard offering, while students who prefer animal-based products will need to actively opt in.
The decision follows a petition launched by the student union at the beginning of February, which gathered more than 950 signatures within a week. Matthew Rochford, a PhD student who supported the motion, described the outcome as a recognition of the environmental damage linked to animal agriculture and said he hoped the university would set an example for other institutions.
The move aligns Southampton with more than 20 universities across the UK that have adopted similar plant-based catering policies, influenced in part by campaigning from the international student network Plant-Based Universitie

item.title: Rising Sigilkore Rapper Luci4 Dies at 23 in Los Angeles
item.description:
Rapper Luci4, born James Dear and also known by the stage name Axxturel, has died at the age of 23. According to a report from the Los Angeles County Medical Examiner, he passed away on Sunday, February 22, at a private residence in Los Angeles. His grandparents told TMZ that he died at a friend’s house, and authorities reportedly found his wallet empty when they arrived at the scene. The Los Angeles Fire Department pronounced him dead at 11:40 a.m., and the Los Angeles Police Department has launched an investigation into the circumstances surrounding his death.
Luci4 was considered a rising talent in the emerging microgenre known as sigilkore, a substyle of trap music that blends electronic elements with cloud rap influences. He first gained significant attention in 2021 through TikTok, where his music quickly resonated with a young audience. His growing popularity led to a record deal with Atlantic Records.
His breakout single, “BodyPartz,” achieved major commercial success and was

item.title: Actor Robert Carradine dies at 71 after long struggle with bipolar disorder
item.description:
Robert Carradine, the American actor widely recognised for his roles in the 1980s comedy franchise 'Revenge of the Nerds' and the Disney Channel series 'Lizzie McGuire', has died at the age of 71. In a statement released shortly after his death, his family confirmed that Carradine took his own life following a nearly two-decade struggle with bipolar disorder. They said they chose to disclose the cause of death to help combat the stigma surrounding mental illness and to encourage open conversations about it.
Carradine was born on 24 March 1954 into a prominent acting family. He was the youngest son of actor John Carradine and the brother of actors David and Keith Carradine. Encouraged by his brother David, he began his film career with a role in the 1972 John Wayne film 'The Cowboys.' Over the years, he built a diverse acting résumé, appearing in notable films such as Martin Scorsese’s 'Mean Streets' (1973), the Oscar-winning 'Coming Home' (1978) alongside Jane Fonda, 'The Long Riders'

item.title: Families of Children with Special Educational Needs Worry About SEND Reform Impact
item.description: Parents of children with high-level special educational needs in England have expressed deep concern over upcoming changes to the Special Educational Needs and Disabilities (SEND) system. From 2035, only children with the most complex needs will be eligible for Education, Health and Care Plans (EHCPs). Current EHCP holders will retain their support until at least September 2029, when reassessments will occur at the end of primary school and GCSEs. Robyn and Adam, parents of eight-year-old Oli, who has autism with Pathological Demand Avoidance (PDA), shared their struggles with their son's behaviour and the challenges of obtaining suitable support. Oli stopped attending school due to anxiety, and the family relies on a visiting education service for a few hours weekly. Many parents report feeling ignored by councils, citing slow communication and staff under pressure. The changes are intended to make mainstream schools more inclusive, but families fear increased isolation and stress for

item.title: Foreign Patients Increasingly Visit Chinese Hospitals for Affordable and Efficient Medical Care
item.description: In recent years, China has become a popular destination for international medical tourists seeking affordable and fast healthcare. Cities like Shenzhen and Shanghai have seen significant increases in foreign patient visits. In 2024, Shenzhen recorded 770,000 visits from overseas patients, while 13 top hospitals in Shanghai reported a 15 percent rise, totaling nearly 270,000 foreign patients. Social media platforms such as TikTok highlight this trend, with many foreigners sharing their experiences of cost-effective and efficient medical care in China. One major factor attracting visitors is the dramatic price difference: a heart checkup that could cost up to $20,000 in the US costs around $75 in China, and simple X-rays can be cheaper than toy doctor kits in Western stores. British influencer Amy, for example, received a diagnosis for a two-year undiagnosed stomach issue on the first day of her visit. Popular medical services among tourists include dentistry, optometry, and Traditional

item.title: GP Practices to Receive Incentives Under New NHS Scheme to Expand Access to Weight Loss Injections
item.description:
GP practices in England are set to receive annual bonuses of up to £3,000 for improving obesity care, including prescribing weight loss injections where clinically appropriate. The initiative forms part of a new GP contract and is supported by £25 million in ring-fenced funding. Health Secretary Wes Streeting said the policy aims to ensure access to obesity treatments is determined by medical need rather than patients’ ability to pay. Currently, although the NHS began rolling out weight loss injections last summer, most of the estimated 2.4 million Britons using these drugs obtain them privately.
Under the new scheme, GP practices must meet specific criteria established by the Department of Health and Social Care to qualify for the financial incentive. The NHS rollout of the drug Mounjaro is expected to be phased in over as long as 12 years. Initially, the injections are available only to patients who are severely obese and have additional health conditions. Around 220,000 patients ha

item.title: Coventry Charity Struggles to Stay Open Amid Declining Donations
item.description: Clothing Coventry, a charity that provides free clothing to vulnerable people and families, is facing possible closure unless it secures additional funding. Established in 2020, the organization relies on donations and currently needs £50,000 annually to cover rent and overhead costs. Founder Katie Wilson highlighted the need for more regular contributors, noting that only 12 people currently donate £5 a month, which is insufficient to sustain operations. The charity assists not only individuals struggling with the cost of living but also statutory services like police and social services in emergency situations, including helping young people in urgent need. Donations are also crucial for unexpected costs, such as the £6,000 heater replacement required last year to ensure volunteers could continue sorting clothes. Beneficiaries, like Mohammed, a recent immigrant from West Africa, have expressed gratitude for the charity’s support, which provides essential clothing and dignity. Partner

item.title: Mexico Deploys Thousands of Soldiers Following Death of Drug Lord El Mencho
item.description: Following the death of Nemesis Oseguera Cervantes, known as 'El Mencho', Mexican authorities have significantly increased military presence in parts of the country to curb violence. Captured by Mexican special forces in Jalisco, El Mencho died in custody, prompting concern over potential unrest. Defence Secretary Ricardo Trevilla confirmed that an additional 2,500 soldiers were sent to western Mexico, raising the total deployed since Sunday to 9,500. The BBC's Will Grant reports from Guadalajara, the capital of Jalisco, highlighting the tense atmosphere and public apprehension amid these security measures. The deployment underscores the ongoing challenges Mexico faces in controlling drug-related violence and maintaining stability in regions affected by organized crime. This news is part of broader coverage of Latin America, where security concerns, environmental events, and political developments are frequently reported by the BBC. The article situates the unrest within the larger cont

item.title: Former British Ambassador Peter Mandelson Arrested Amid Misconduct Investigation
item.description: Peter Mandelson, the former British ambassador to the United States and ex-government minister, has been arrested on suspicion of misconduct in public office. Authorities are investigating allegations that he may have shared sensitive government information with Jeffrey Epstein during his ministerial tenure. Mandelson has not publicly commented on the arrest, but sources indicate he maintains that he acted lawfully and was not motivated by financial gain. The arrest comes amid ongoing scrutiny by the Metropolitan Police, which launched the investigation earlier this month. In parallel news, the UK government has unveiled reforms to the system for children with special educational needs and disabilities in England. The education secretary, Bridget Phillipson, announced these changes, which were subsequently analyzed by BBC education editor Branwen Jeffreys. The reforms are intended to improve support for children requiring additional educational assistance. The Newscast episode covering

item.title: Two Young Men from Dereham Die on Snowdon, Remembered Fondly by Tutors
item.description: Two friends from Dereham, Norfolk, Eddie Hill, 20, and Jayden Long, 19, were found dead on Wales's highest mountain, Yr Wyddfa (Snowdon), after concerns about their welfare were raised. Their bodies were discovered by a mountain rescue team on Thursday on Crib y Ddysgl, a ridge near the summit, following a search in challenging winter conditions. The pair, who left Dereham Sixth Form College in 2024, were remembered by former tutors as charismatic, friendly, and full of potential. Kirsty Brook, head of year 13, highlighted their energy and enthusiasm, saying they could have achieved anything due to their personality and drive. Teachers noted that their presence was deeply felt in classrooms and staff rooms, emphasizing the impact they had during their short lives. Eddie had worked at Slater Menswear, and Jayden was planning to pursue an apprenticeship. The tragic deaths have led to inquests opening on Friday. The local community and college staff have expressed shock and sadness at the

item.title: British Woman Faces Charges After Reporting Alleged Rape in Hong Kong
item.description: Isabel Rose, a 25-year-old British woman from Hackney, London, reported an alleged rape to Hong Kong police in early 2024 after meeting a man she had previously encountered while traveling in Asia. The man was arrested but later released without charges. Shortly after, Rose was charged with blackmail and perverting the course of justice, accused of demanding money from the individual she reported. Rose denies these allegations, maintaining that she was seeking compensation initially offered following the alleged assault. She described being handcuffed to a hospital bed and monitored by police during the investigation. Throughout the legal proceedings, Rose has been on bail, unable to work or leave Hong Kong, and supported by her mother, who traveled to assist her. Legal experts note that cases where an accuser becomes the accused are rare, highlighting challenges for sexual assault survivors in Hong Kong. NGOs warn that such cases deter survivors from reporting crimes and place immense

item.title: UK government proposes ban on unlicensed betting firms sponsoring football clubs
item.description: The UK government is considering a ban on sports sponsorship by gambling companies that do not hold a UK licence, aiming to protect consumers and uphold regulatory standards. Ministers have expressed concerns about unlicensed operators failing to conduct mandatory financial vulnerability checks, follow responsible advertising rules, or implement adequate data protection measures, leaving customers exposed to fraud and identity theft. Unlicensed gambling has also been linked to organised crime. A consultation on the proposals is set to launch this spring. Previously, several Premier League and Championship clubs, including Bournemouth, Fulham, Newcastle, Wolves, and Burnley, were warned by the Gambling Commission over their association with TGP Europe, which had surrendered its UK licence after breaching anti-money laundering rules. Clubs received letters warning they could face prosecution if they continued promoting unlicensed operators. Culture Secretary Lisa Nandy emphasized that fa

item.title: UK Chancellor to Deliver Spring Statement on Economic Forecasts
item.description: Chancellor Rachel Reeves will deliver the UK’s 2026 Spring Statement on 3 March, providing an update on economic forecasts, including growth, inflation, unemployment, government spending, and tax revenue. The Spring Statement, while not as significant as the annual Budget, informs government policy decisions on potential tax or spending changes. The Office for Budget Responsibility (OBR), an independent body that monitors government finances, will publish its latest forecasts following Reeves’ speech. The OBR typically evaluates whether the government will meet its fiscal rules—such as avoiding borrowing for day-to-day spending and reducing debt relative to national income—but this year, assessments will occur only at the autumn Budget. Although no major policy announcements are expected in the Spring Statement, changes to benefits or minor adjustments could still be outlined. Economic indicators suggest modest growth, with GDP up 0.1% in the last quarter of 2025 and 1.3% over the year

item.title: UK Company Director Sentenced for Supplying Aircraft Engine Parts with Forged Safety Documents
item.description:
A British company director has been sentenced to four years and eight months in prison after admitting to a major fraud involving aircraft engine parts worth nearly £7 million. Jose Alejandro Zamora Yrala, 38, ran AOG Technics, a company that supplied tens of thousands of components for CFM56 aircraft engines between 2019 and 2023. These engines are widely used on Boeing 737 and Airbus A320 aircraft, making the case particularly serious for global aviation safety.
Southwark Crown Court heard that approximately 60,000 parts were distributed with falsified documentation, including forged Authorised Release Certificates (ARCs), which are required to confirm that aviation components are safe and airworthy. Some certificates were altered versions of genuine documents obtained through an accomplice working as an airline technician, while others were entirely fabricated with the assistance of a Spanish graphic designer. Prosecutors also revealed that Zamora Yrala created fake employee identi

item.title: Troy Deeney's Premier League Team of the Week Selections
item.description: After each round of Premier League fixtures, football pundit Troy Deeney selects his team and manager of the week. This week, Dean Henderson of Crystal Palace earned recognition for a penalty save and keeping a clean sheet against Wolves. In defense, Virgil van Dijk of Liverpool was praised for his consistency and leadership, while Harry Maguire of Manchester United was highlighted as a reliable presence in a team enjoying a strong run. Lewis Hall impressed with his performance against Manchester City, and Nico O'Reilly of Manchester City was named player of the weekend for his impactful midfield display and versatility, contributing two notable goals. Anton Stach of Leeds stood out with a key free-kick against Aston Villa. Eberechi Eze's two goals in the north London derby secured his spot, while Diego Gomez helped Brighton to their first league win in seven games. Zian Flemming of Burnley displayed a disruptive, classic striker performance against Chelsea. Viktor Gyokeres also scored

item.title: Menna Fitzpatrick Returns from Injury to Join Great Britain Squad for Winter Paralympics
item.description:
Great Britain’s most decorated Winter Paralympian, Menna Fitzpatrick, has been selected to compete at the upcoming Winter Paralympics in Milan-Cortina, despite suffering a significant knee injury in December. The 27-year-old para-alpine skier chose not to undergo surgery in order to keep her hopes of competing alive, demonstrating her determination to participate in the Games, which begin on 6 March and run until 15 March. Fitzpatrick, who has won six Paralympic medals, will compete alongside her guide, Katie Guest, as part of the second wave of athletes named to the British team.
The para-alpine squad also includes Fred Warburton and guide James Hannan, Sam Cozens and guide Adam Hall, Hester Poole and guide Ali Hall, as well as Dominic Allen. In para-snowboard, Davy Zyw has been selected and is believed to be the first snowsport athlete with motor neurone disease (MND) to compete at the Winter Paralympics. Zyw expressed immense pride at his selection, describing his journey to the Ga

item.title: FedEx Seeks Refund of Trump-Era Tariffs Following Supreme Court Ruling
item.description: FedEx has filed a lawsuit seeking a full refund of tariffs imposed under former President Donald Trump's emergency powers. The tariffs, introduced last April on imports from most countries through the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA), were recently ruled illegal by the US Supreme Court. The company is suing US Customs and Border Protection (CBP), its commissioner Rodney Scott, and the US government, aiming to recover all duties paid. FedEx stated it has taken steps to protect its rights as an importer of record. Following the Supreme Court's decision, 22 Democratic Senators proposed legislation requiring full refunds, including interest, to be issued within 180 days, prioritizing small businesses. The Trump administration had collected at least $130 billion in additional revenue through these tariffs. While the ruling invalidates the tariffs, it does not provide clear instructions on how refunds should be processed. Several companies, including Revlon, Alcoa, Bumble

item.title: Europe Expands Orbital Launch Capabilities from Arctic and Northern Spaceports
item.description: The Esrange Space Centre in Swedish Lapland, run by the Swedish Space Corporation (SSC), is emerging as a key site in Europe's expanding orbital launch ambitions. Known for over 600 sub-orbital rocket launches since the 1960s, Esrange has now inaugurated a new launchpad for satellite missions, hosting clients like South Korea's Perigee and US-based Firefly Aerospace. The centre aims to provide orbital launches for satellites into polar orbit, leveraging its northern location and favorable weather conditions. The expansion includes infrastructure tailored to Firefly's Alpha rocket and is supported by a technology safeguard agreement between Sweden and the US. Other European spaceports, such as SaxaVord in Scotland, Andøya in Norway, and Santa Maria in the Azores, are also competing in the commercial launch market. Despite setbacks for companies like Virgin Orbit and Orbex, experts believe multiple sites will succeed due to growing satellite demand driven by communications, internet, and

item.title: New Zealand Supports UK Proposal to Remove Prince Andrew from Succession Line
item.description: New Zealand has expressed its support for the possible removal of Prince Andrew from the royal line of succession, should the UK government decide to proceed. Prime Minister Christopher Luxon's spokesperson stated that New Zealand would back such a move. This follows a similar announcement by Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese, who confirmed his government's agreement with any UK proposal concerning Andrew, in light of his alleged ties to the convicted paedophile financier Jeffrey Epstein. Andrew was recently arrested on suspicion of misconduct in public office, allegations he denies. The UK government is considering legislation to remove him from the line of succession, a process that would require approval from all 14 Commonwealth countries where King Charles III is head of state, including Australia and New Zealand. Andrew remains eighth in line despite being stripped of his royal titles, including 'prince,' last October. While the New Zealand deputy prime minister David Sey

item.title: Trump Organization Announces $1bn Gold Coast Skyscraper Project Amid Conflict of Interest Concerns
item.description:
The Trump Organization has announced plans to build a 335-metre skyscraper on Queensland’s Gold Coast, which would become Australia’s tallest building if completed as proposed. The project, named Trump International Hotel & Tower, Gold Coast, is expected to cost at least $1bn (£740m) and construction is scheduled to begin in August, according to developer Altus Property Group. The 91-storey tower will include 285 hotel rooms, 272 luxury residential apartments, retail outlets, restaurants and an exclusive beach club along the beachfront. Eric Trump, executive vice president of the Trump Organization and son of US President Donald Trump, described the development as bringing the prestige of a world-class luxury brand to Australia.
However, the project may face competition from another proposed development on the same stretch of coastline. The twin-tower One Park Lane project is planned to rise 50 metres higher, potentially surpassing the Trump-branded building as the tallest in the cou

item.title: Russian soldiers testify to BBC about commanders executing troops for refusing orders in Ukraine war
item.description: In a BBC documentary titled 'The Zero Line: Inside Russia's War', two Russian soldiers, Ilya and Dima, who have fled the country, described witnessing severe brutality within their own military on the front lines in Ukraine. Ilya, a 35-year-old former teacher of children with special needs who was drafted, recounted seeing a commander shoot four soldiers at point-blank range after they fled the front line and refused to return. This practice, known in Russian military slang as 'zeroing', serves as punishment for refusing orders and as intimidation to deter others from similar actions. Dima also witnessed such executions, describing them as routine and stating that commanders killing their own men had become 'a normal thing'. He vividly recalled the close-range shootings, emphasizing the stark reality of the acts—'just click, clack, bang'—and not dramatized like in films. The soldiers spoke from an undisclosed location outside Russia. The documentary highlights broader horrors, includin

item.title: US Women’s Olympic Hockey Team Declines State of the Union Invitation Due to Schedule Conflicts
item.description: The gold medal-winning U.S. women’s Olympic hockey team has declined an invitation to attend President Donald Trump’s State of the Union address, citing prior scheduling conflicts with academic and professional commitments following the Olympics. The invitation came after Trump celebrated the men’s team’s Olympic victory by phone, even offering a military plane for their travel to Washington, D.C., and mentioning that the women’s team should attend as well. Members of the women’s team were formally invited, but they issued a statement expressing gratitude for the recognition while noting they could not participate due to their commitments. The men’s team, which also won gold against Canada, has not confirmed whether they will attend. Social media reacted strongly to the interaction between Trump and the women’s team during the call. The U.S. women’s hockey team, who also won gold in PyeongChang during Trump’s first term, now return to the Professional Women’s Hockey League, while the m

item.title: Mother of Terminally Ill Teen Supports Assisted Dying Debate as Senedd Prepares to Vote
item.description:
The mother of a 19-year-old terminally ill teenager has said she now supports changes to UK law that would allow assisted dying, after witnessing her son’s prolonged suffering from an inoperable brain tumour. Shelley Herniman, from Chepstow, had initially opposed her son Noah’s wish for an assisted death following his diagnosis during the Covid pandemic. However, after more than a year of intensive chemotherapy and severe side effects that left him weighing just 15kg at age 15, requiring feeding tubes and a wheelchair, she says her perspective has changed.
Noah’s treatment lasted 74 weeks and could not be completed because his body could not withstand further chemotherapy. His mother described the physical toll on his organs and the extreme pain he endured. While she says she hopes never to face the decision, she does not want her son to suffer unnecessarily or be forced to travel abroad alone to end his life if his condition deteriorates beyond help.
The debate comes as Welsh politi

item.title: Police officer killed and two injured in explosion outside Moscow's Savyolovsky Station
item.description:
An explosion outside Savyolovsky Station Square in Moscow has left one police officer dead and two others injured, according to Russia's Interior Ministry. The incident occurred shortly after midnight local time on Tuesday, when officers from the north-eastern district of Moscow were patrolling the area in their vehicle. Authorities reported that an unidentified explosive device was detonated by an unknown individual as the officers carried out their duties.
Initial statements suggested that a male suspect had fled the scene, but officials later confirmed that the alleged attacker died at the location of the blast. Investigators have not yet disclosed details about the suspect’s identity or the type of explosive device used. A criminal case has been opened in connection with the attack, including charges related to the attempted murder of a law enforcement officer and the illegal use of an explosive device. Law enforcement agencies are working together to determine the full circumstan

item.title: Trump Implements 10% Global Tariff Following Supreme Court Ruling
item.description: US President Donald Trump has implemented a 10% global tariff after the Supreme Court blocked many of his previous sweeping import taxes. Following last week's ruling, Trump signed an executive order to impose the new levy starting on 24 February, citing Section 122 of the 1974 Trade Act, which allows him to enact such measures for 150 days without congressional approval. The administration stated the temporary duty is intended to address international payment imbalances and strengthen trade relations in favor of American workers, farmers, and manufacturers. Trump has claimed tariffs are necessary to reduce the US trade deficit, which recently reached approximately $1.2 trillion, a 2.1% increase compared to 2024. The US has already collected around $130 billion in tariffs under the 1977 International Emergency Economic Powers Act. The Supreme Court, in a 6-3 decision, ruled that Trump had exceeded his authority with previous global tariffs. Following the decision, Trump warned he might

item.title: Aston Martin Faces Major Challenges with New F1 Car and Honda Engine
item.description: Aston Martin entered the 2026 Formula 1 season with high expectations, including the arrival of design legend Adrian Newey, a state-of-the-art factory, a strong sponsorship portfolio, and two-time world champion Fernando Alonso behind the wheel. Early testing, however, exposed significant problems: the car struggled for reliability, completed minimal laps, and proved uncompetitive compared to rivals. Delays in development were compounded by Newey joining late in March 2025, after the aerodynamic regulations were finalized, forcing a redesign of the car and leaving Aston Martin months behind competitors. Internal leadership instability, including recent executive changes, has also contributed to the team's difficulties. Complicating matters, Honda's engine program has fallen short, reminiscent of its early struggles with McLaren in 2015–2017. Despite prior F1 experience and involvement in regulatory planning, Honda lacked a fully dedicated team to design and build the new power unit, af

item.title: Ukraine’s Resilience Amidst Drone Warfare and Russian Advances
item.description: In eastern Ukraine, particularly Donetsk and Slovyansk, the war has entered a new phase dominated by advanced drone warfare, transforming traditional front-line combat. Ukrainian forces have adapted by using nets to protect roads from explosive drones, many of which were donated by European fishermen, and by employing FPV drones with sophisticated surveillance systems. Both Ukraine and Russia are now among the world's most experienced in drone warfare, leveraging technologies like Starlink for battlefield communications. Despite pressure from the US and European leaders to agree to ceasefires or territorial concessions, President Volodymyr Zelensky remains steadfast in refusing to surrender land that remains under Ukrainian control. Civilians and volunteers continue to navigate extreme dangers, from delivering aid to collecting fallen soldiers’ remains. Slovyansk and Kramatorsk are fortified as defensive strongholds, while life in Donetsk remains perilous. The war has claimed tens of t

item.title: Zero-hours contracts reach record levels as government plans worker protections
item.description: The number of workers on zero-hours contracts in the UK has reached a record 1.23 million, driven largely by young people aged 16 to 24 and those not in full-time education. Analysis by the Work Foundation at Lancaster University, using Office for National Statistics data, shows an increase of 91,000 compared to the previous year. Women make up 54% of these workers, and a record 32.8% rely on such contracts for full-time work, often seeking additional hours or secondary jobs. Zero-hours contracts provide no guaranteed work or income, leaving workers with unpredictable schedules and financial uncertainty. Labour's 2024 election manifesto includes plans to ban exploitative zero-hours contracts and introduce rights to guaranteed hours, reasonable notice, and compensation for last-minute cancellations. Trade unions, including the TUC, have called for these measures to be implemented quickly to improve workers’ security and living standards. Critics argue that zero-hours contracts exacerbat

item.title: Train CCTV Failures Hinder Investigation of Sexual Offences on UK Rail Network
item.description: A BBC investigation has revealed that sexual offenders on trains in England, Scotland, and Wales are often going untraced due to widespread issues with CCTV on the rail network. British Transport Police (BTP) officers report difficulties in collecting usable evidence, with many incidents not recorded, cameras malfunctioning, or footage being overwritten. One woman, Beth Wright, experienced harassment on a London Tube train, but her attackers could not be identified because of broken cameras. Similarly, Rebecca Horne faced sexual assault on a train, and although her attacker was eventually caught, BTP had no CCTV evidence initially. Investigations show that some train companies have cameras out of service for extended periods, with one company having a camera inactive for over five months. Rail operators are not legally required to have functioning CCTV, and footage retention periods are often very short, sometimes just 48 hours. Transport for London also lacks sufficient CCTV on older

item.title: Russian soldiers report witnessing executions and brutal conditions on the front lines in Ukraine
item.description: Four Russian soldiers have provided detailed testimony to the BBC about extreme violence and abuse they witnessed on the front lines in Ukraine. According to the accounts, commanders ordered executions of soldiers who refused orders, a practice known as 'zeroing,' and carried out what are described as brutal 'meat storm' attacks, where waves of men were sent into near-suicide assaults. The soldiers describe being tortured, electrocuted, and humiliated for refusing participation, and report seeing the bodies of fellow troops dumped in pits after execution. Some commanders involved, such as Alexei Ksenofontov, have received state awards despite these alleged abuses. The witnesses describe the psychological and physical toll of the war, including their own trauma from seeing and surviving such violence. The Russian government denies independent verification of these accounts, asserting its forces operate with restraint. This report highlights a breakdown of law and discipline within Russi

item.title: Matty Healy and Gabbriette Bechtel reportedly plan July wedding in California
item.description: According to a report by The Sun, Matty Healy, lead singer of The 1975, is reportedly set to marry model and singer Gabbriette Bechtel in July 2026 in California. This potential wedding would occur just weeks after the alleged wedding date of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce, which The Sun states is scheduled for June 13 in Watch Hill, Rhode Island, although neither Swift nor Kelce has publicly confirmed any wedding plans. Healy and Swift were briefly linked in 2023, with their relationship reportedly lasting about two months, sparking fan speculation and discussions, especially after the release of Swift’s album 'The Tortured Poets Department'. Following the split with Swift, Healy began dating Bechtel in 2023. A friend told The Sun that the couple aims for a lavish celebration, with no expense spared, emphasizing their excitement and party spirit. If the reported dates are accurate, both Swift and Healy could be having ceremonies within weeks of one another this summer. However, as neit

item.title: Dakota Johnson reflects on her past relationship with Chris Martin and moves forward
item.description: Dakota Johnson has reportedly moved on from her on-and-off relationship with Coldplay singer Chris Martin, which lasted for eight years and ended last year. According to sources, Johnson regrets giving Martin multiple chances during their relationship and feels she stayed longer than she should have after their connection turned toxic. Friends of the actor had expressed concerns that she was still vulnerable to Martin, but Johnson has reportedly made it clear she is ready to move forward. Recently, Johnson has been linked to musician Tucker Pillsbury, known professionally as Role Model. Sources say she is enjoying her time with Pillsbury and that the relationship has helped her gain perspective on what she had been missing while still involved with Martin. Though she reportedly still cares about her former partner, Johnson has made it clear that she does not intend to rekindle their romance. The new relationship appears to be giving her the support and distraction she needed to fully m

item.title: Practical Strategies for Combining Comfort and Style in Home Design
item.description: This article explores how to harmonize aesthetics and functionality in home design by prioritizing comfort alongside visual appeal. It emphasizes the importance of understanding how people experience a space throughout the day, noting temperature swings, airflow, and odors that photos cannot capture. Mapping comfort issues, such as drafts, hot or cold spots, and pressure imbalances, is recommended before choosing furniture or layouts. Proper placement and design of HVAC components, including vents, returns, and thermostats, are essential to maintain both performance and appearance. The piece also highlights material choices that respond to humidity, protect finishes, and integrate biophilic design elements to create healthier, more resilient spaces. Zoning, smart controls, and attention to air circulation ensure that rooms are comfortable without constant manual adjustments. Regular maintenance, including filter changes and duct sealing, prevents system inefficiencies that can disrupt

item.title: 2026 Met Gala Theme Revealed: Fashion as Art
item.description: The 2026 Met Gala, scheduled for Monday, May 4, will celebrate the Costume Institute's spring exhibition, “Costume Art,” at the Metropolitan Museum of Art. This year’s dress code, “Fashion is Art,” invites attendees to explore how designers use the human body as a canvas, reflecting the exhibition's focus on the centrality of the dressed body. The exhibition will feature nearly 400 objects, including garments from the Costume Institute alongside paintings, sculptures, and artworks spanning 5,000 years, and will be displayed in the new Condé M. Nast Galleries near the Great Hall. Curator Andrew Bolton emphasizes that fashion connects every department and gallery in the museum, reinforcing the idea of the dressed body as a unifying theme. The gala’s co-chairs include Beyoncé, Nicole Kidman, Venus Williams, and Anna Wintour, with host committee members such as Anthony Vaccarello, Zoë Kravitz, Sabrina Carpenter, Doja Cat, and Misty Copeland. Additional honorary chairs are Jeff Bezos and La

item.title: 16-year-old convicted for membership in banned neo-Nazi organisation
item.description: A 16-year-old boy from Northumberland has been found guilty of being a member of the banned extremist group The Base and of possessing and sharing terrorist publications. Leeds Crown Court delivered a unanimous verdict on these charges, although jurors could not reach a decision on the more serious accusation of preparing acts of terrorism, and no retrial will occur. Police raided the teenager's home in February, uncovering an array of weapons including a crossbow, knives, and a gas-powered air pistol. Authorities also discovered Nazi memorabilia, posters linked to The Base, and a full-sized skeleton with a mask in his bedroom. The boy, who was 15 at the time of his arrest, had been active in extreme right-wing online chat groups on platforms such as Telegram, Snapchat, TikTok, and Wire. According to Counter Terror Policing North East, he created an online persona to escape reality, with his social media use escalating through his childhood. The Base promotes a white supremacist ideolo

item.title: Analysis of the Causes and Progress of Russia's Invasion of Ukraine
item.description: In 2022, Russian President Vladimir Putin ordered a large-scale invasion of Ukraine, aiming to capture Kyiv, remove its pro-Western government, and reassert Russia's influence over the country. This followed earlier moves, including the 2014 annexation of Crimea and the support of separatists in the eastern Donbas region. Putin framed the invasion as a 'demilitarization and denazification' campaign, a claim widely rejected by historians and international bodies, given Ukraine's democratic government and the Jewish heritage of President Volodymyr Zelensky. Russia also opposed Ukraine's potential membership in NATO, viewing Western support and military aid as a security threat. Despite extensive military operations, Ukraine has retained much of its territory, while Russia holds only a portion, and the war has become a prolonged conflict with significant civilian and military casualties on both sides. Efforts at peace talks, including US-brokered summits and proposals from former US Presi

item.title: Computational Study Finds 40,000-Year-Old Stone Age Sign Sequences Comparable in Complexity to Proto-Cuneiform
item.description:
A new study by linguist Christian Bentz of Saarland University and archaeologist Ewa Dutkiewicz from the Museum of Prehistory and Early History in Berlin suggests that sign sequences carved into Stone Age objects over 40,000 years ago may represent an early system of visual information encoding. The research, soon to be published in the journal PNAS, analyzed more than 3,000 geometric signs found on around 260 Paleolithic artefacts dating from 34,000 to 45,000 years ago.
Many of these objects were discovered in caves in the Swabian Jura region of southwestern Germany, including the Vogelherd Cave, Geißenklösterle Cave, and Hohlenstein-Stadel Cave. Artefacts such as a mammoth figurine carved from ivory and the so-called “Adorant” plate depicting a lion-human hybrid feature repeated patterns of crosses, dots, lines, and notches. The researchers used computational methods, statistical modeling, and machine learning to examine the frequency, repetition, and predictability of these signs.

item.title: Life Under Fire in Belgorod: How the Ukraine War Has Reached Russia’s Border City
item.description:
Four years after Vladimir Putin launched Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, the war has reshaped daily life not only in Ukraine but also inside Russia itself, particularly in the border city of Belgorod. Located around 25 miles from the Ukrainian border, Belgorod has become a frequent target of Ukrainian drone and missile strikes. Once far removed from the battlefield, the city is now dotted with bomb shelters, anti-drone nets, barricaded bus stops, and experiences recurring blackouts.
Residents describe living under constant threat. An elderly couple narrowly survived when a drone crashed into their apartment, causing extensive damage. Air raid sirens are now a routine part of life. A Sky News team also experienced a drone strike firsthand while traveling near the border town of Shebekino, highlighting the persistent dangers in the region.
According to local authorities, at least 440 people have been killed in the Belgorod region since the war began. Though this number is far

item.title: UK Police Forces Continue Using Microsoft Copilot After AI Error in Football Fan Ban Case
item.description:
At least 21 police forces across England continue to use Microsoft’s Copilot AI tool despite a major controversy involving West Midlands Police (WMP), which blocked access to the software after it generated inaccurate information used in an intelligence report. The error contributed to a decision to ban Maccabi Tel Aviv fans from attending a match against Aston Villa in November. The AI tool produced a so-called “hallucination,” referencing a match that had never taken place, and this incorrect information was included in documentation justifying the ban.
The incident became one of the most significant policing controversies of the past year and ultimately led to the departure of WMP Chief Constable Craig Guildford under government pressure. The Home Affairs Select Committee later raised further concerns after Copilot generated inaccurate claims about past disorder linked to a separate match in Amsterdam in 2024.
While some forces, including those in Scotland, Northern Ireland, North

item.title: Drone Warfare and Electronic Networks Reshape the Front Lines in Ukraine
item.description:
The article describes how the war in Ukraine has transformed into a highly technological and electronically driven conflict, where drones and communication networks dominate the battlefield. The front line is portrayed as a 1,200-kilometer stretch with a 'grey zone' between 20 and 40 kilometers wide. Alongside traditional trenches, the area is now covered with fishing nets and extensive fiber optic cables, reflecting the growing importance of counter-drone measures and secure communications.
According to the report, modern warfare in Ukraine increasingly depends on the quality of the connection between drone operators and their devices. Aerial, naval, and ground drones are central to combat operations, and both sides are engaged in constant innovation to improve performance and autonomy. The article emphasizes the rapid pace of technological change, noting that artificial intelligence is becoming essential to guide and target drones more efficiently, reducing reliance on human operato

item.title: Overview of the Current Situation in the Ukraine-Russia Conflict
item.description: The conflict in Ukraine is approaching its fifth year, with Russian forces gradually expanding control, primarily in eastern regions such as Luhansk and Donetsk (the Donbas), as well as attempting advances in Zaporizhzhia and Kherson. Analysts estimate Russia captured about 4,700 sq km in 2025, though Moscow claims 6,000 sq km. Ukraine has reportedly lost around 55,000 soldiers, while nearly 160,000 Russian casualties have been confirmed. Strategic towns like Pokrovsk and northern Kupyansk remain key battlegrounds, with Russia seeking to create buffer zones and gain artillery reach near Kharkiv. Ukraine’s defensive measures, including the Donetsk 'fortress belt' and Western-supplied weapons, have slowed Russian advances. Both sides continue to launch strikes on military and infrastructure targets, including deep strikes by Ukraine into Russia to disrupt its war economy. Peace talks involving the US have made limited progress on military issues, but territorial disagreements remain unre

item.title: Global Report Highlights Concentration of Geopolitical Cyberattacks in Ten Key Countries
item.description:
A recent annual Global Cyber Threat Report published by Radware indicates that cyberattacks have become a central indirect instrument in modern geopolitical conflicts. According to the report, 80 percent of politically motivated cyberattacks are concentrated in just ten countries, demonstrating a high degree of geographic focus. Israel tops the list with 1,881 claimed attacks in the past year, accounting for 12.2 percent of the global total. The surge is largely linked to ongoing tensions involving Hamas, Iran, and aligned groups, with pro-Iranian cells reportedly claiming responsibility for many of the incidents.
The United States and Ukraine follow, with 1,448 and 1,373 attacks respectively, reflecting their exposure to strategic rivalries in the digital domain. Germany and France also appear prominently, with 849 and 840 attacks, separated by only a narrow margin. Other countries in the top ten include Thailand, Italy, Spain, India, and Poland, illustrating the global scope of cybe

item.title: Authorities Investigate Canine Distemper Outbreak Killing 72 Tigers at Chiang Mai Park
item.description: Authorities in Chiang Mai, northern Thailand, are investigating the deaths of 72 tigers at Tiger Kingdom Chiang Mai, a popular tourist attraction where visitors can interact with the animals. The deaths occurred in under two weeks across the park’s two facilities, which house over 240 tigers. Initial tests indicated the tigers were infected with canine distemper virus, a highly contagious disease that primarily affects dogs but can be fatal for big cats, attacking the respiratory, gastrointestinal, and nervous systems. Some samples also tested positive for bacteria linked to respiratory disease, and preliminary findings suggested feline parvovirus might have been involved. Authorities have cremated and buried the deceased tigers and temporarily closed the park for disinfection. Animal rights organizations have criticized the living conditions of captive tigers in Thailand, highlighting the vulnerability of such facilities to infectious disease. Previous outbreaks, such as the 2004 Chon

item.title: Peter Mandelson Released on Bail Following Misconduct Investigation
item.description: Lord Peter Mandelson, a former British politician, was released on bail by the Metropolitan Police following his arrest on suspicion of misconduct in public office. The 72-year-old returned to his London residence around 2:00 GMT. His release comes amid ongoing investigations into alleged wrongdoing. Similarly, Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, a former prince, was arrested on suspicion of misconduct in public office and spent a day at a Norfolk police station before being released under investigation. Unmarked police vehicles were also observed arriving at the Sandringham Estate, where Mountbatten-Windsor resides. The news has generated significant public and media attention, including reactions at London Fashion Week, where crowds and journalists gathered hours after the arrest. The article also briefly touches on other unrelated news items, including a cargo ship accident in the North Sea, Queen Camilla praising the bravery of the Hunt family, New Year Honours recognitions for Torvill and

item.title: Russia Links Attempted Assassination of General Alexeiev to International Involvement
item.description: On February 6, 2026, an attempt was made on the life of Russian General Vladimir Alexeiev, who was shot multiple times but survived after being hospitalized and undergoing surgery. Russian authorities, including the Federal Security Service (FSB), quickly identified the shooter, Lioubomir Korba, who was later apprehended in Dubai and extradited to Russia. His accomplices, Viktor Vasin and Zinaida Serebritskaya, were also tracked, with Vasin detained in Moscow and Serebritskaya fleeing to Turkey under an international arrest warrant. Alexander Bortnikov, FSB Director, stated that the attack was sponsored by the Ukrainian intelligence services but included the participation of other countries, notably the United Kingdom, and alleged that Kyiv acted under the guidance of Western intelligence. Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov condemned the attempt as an act of terrorism, suggesting it aimed to disrupt peace negotiations in Ukraine. The FSB continues to investigate and promised to rel

item.title: Severe Winter Storm Hits Northeast United States Causing Travel Bans and School Closures
item.description: An intense winter storm has swept across the northeastern United States, affecting multiple states including New York, Connecticut, and New Jersey. Authorities in New York City have imposed a full travel ban from 9:00 PM local time on Sunday to noon on Monday, closing all streets, highways, and bridges to non-emergency traffic. Mayor Zohran Mamdani announced that schools will remain closed, and residents are advised to stay indoors. The storm, described as the most powerful nor'easter in nearly a decade, is expected to bring heavy snow, strong winds, and coastal flooding. Snow accumulation is reported across affected states, disrupting transportation and daily activities. Emergency services are on high alert, and authorities urge residents to follow safety protocols. The storm's impact is compounded by coastal flooding, especially in low-lying areas. Citizens are advised to monitor local updates and avoid unnecessary travel until conditions improve.

item.title: Community Voices Concerns Over Asylum Seeker Accommodation in Crowborough
item.description: In Crowborough, East Sussex, local residents and campaigners have expressed strong concerns over the Home Office’s decision to convert an army training centre into housing for around 540 asylum seekers. The decision is part of the UK government’s broader plan to phase out expensive hotel accommodation for asylum seekers by 2029 and move them to larger sites or shared housing. Crowborough Shield, a local advocacy group led by campaigner Kim Bailey, has initiated legal action, seeking a judicial review of the Home Secretary’s decision, citing lack of consultation and transparency. Residents have mixed feelings: some show empathy toward asylum seekers seeking sanctuary, while others express fear about potential social issues. For instance, 80-year-old Chris Eustace raised concerns about civil unrest, while Rita Williams mentioned feeling unsafe at night. Local authorities, including Liberal Democrat councillor James Partridge, also criticized the government for forcing the council and res

item.title: Sunlight Creates Fiery Optical Illusion at Yosemite’s Horsetail Fall
item.description:
A seasonal natural phenomenon at Yosemite National Park in California is once again drawing attention as Horsetail Fall appears to glow like molten lava when illuminated by the setting sun. The striking visual effect occurs when the late-February sunset hits the waterfall at just the right angle, casting a vivid orange glow that transforms the cascading water into what looks like flowing lava down the granite face of El Capitan.
According to the US National Park Service (NPS), the phenomenon is visible only under specific conditions. It typically occurs in mid to late February and requires clear skies to allow the sunlight to directly strike the waterfall. Even slight haze or cloud cover can significantly reduce the intensity of the display. Additionally, the waterfall must be actively flowing, which usually happens during winter when snowmelt feeds Horsetail Fall. In drier conditions, the waterfall may slow to a trickle or stop entirely, making the effect impossible to observe.
Hors

item.title: Former Philippine President Duterte Faces ICC Hearing Over Drug War Killings
item.description: Former Philippine president Rodrigo Duterte is facing a pre-trial hearing at the International Criminal Court (ICC) to determine whether he should stand trial for crimes against humanity. Duterte, who governed as both mayor of Davao and president of the Philippines, led a controversial 'war on drugs' that human rights groups claim resulted in tens of thousands of extrajudicial killings, including children. ICC prosecutors have charged him with three counts of crimes against humanity, alleging he was involved in at least 76 murders, and acted as an 'indirect co-perpetrator' by using police and hired enforcers to carry out killings. Duterte has waived his right to appear in court, rejecting the ICC’s authority and maintaining his innocence. The hearing is scheduled over four days, after which judges will decide whether to proceed to a full trial. Duterte’s arrest in The Hague followed a political fallout between his family and President Ferdinand Marcos Jr., breaking a prior alliance. Th

item.title: Bellarmine Mugabe, youngest son of late Zimbabwean president, charged with attempted murder in Johannesburg shooting
item.description: Bellarmine Mugabe, the 28-year-old youngest son of Zimbabwe's late former President Robert Mugabe and his second wife Grace Mugabe, appeared briefly in a South African court in Johannesburg where he was formally charged with attempted murder. The charges stem from an incident last Thursday at a luxury property in the upscale Hyde Park suburb, where a 23-year-old man was allegedly shot in the back during an altercation inside the home. Prosecutors state that the victim attempted to flee but collapsed outside the gate, which was then closed. The victim remains in hospital in critical condition. Mugabe was arrested alongside his 33-year-old bodyguard, Tobias Tamirepi Matonhodze. Police recovered bullet cartridges during a search of the property but have not located the firearm. In addition to attempted murder, both men face charges of defeating the ends of justice related to the missing weapon, as well as other potential counts. Mugabe appeared calm and dressed in black during the hearing

item.title: The Benefits of Buying Windows and Doors from Authorized Dealers
item.description:
The article explains why selecting an authorized dealer for windows and doors is a critical decision for homeowners seeking quality, durability, and long-term value. While many buyers focus on style, materials, or pricing, the source of purchase plays an equally important role. Authorized dealers guarantee genuine products that meet manufacturer standards, protecting customers from counterfeit or substandard goods. This ensures that materials, finishes, and performance features align with brand promises.
Another key advantage highlighted is expert knowledge and professional support. Authorized dealers employ trained staff who understand product specifications, installation requirements, and maintenance guidelines. Their expertise helps customers choose the most suitable options for their projects while avoiding costly mistakes. In addition, many authorized dealers oversee the installation process, ensuring proper fitting and optimal performance.
Warranty protection is another major b

item.title: Trump Warns of Higher Tariffs on Nations Reneging on Recent Trade Agreements After Supreme Court Ruling
item.description: US President Donald Trump has issued a strong warning on Truth Social, threatening significantly higher tariffs on countries that attempt to back away from or renegotiate recent trade deals following a major Supreme Court decision. Last week, the Court ruled that Trump exceeded his authority by imposing broad global tariffs under the 1977 International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA), invalidating much of last year's sweeping levies. In response, Trump swiftly announced a new temporary 10% global tariff using Section 122 of the Trade Act, which he later increased to 15%, set to take effect imminently with some exemptions. This has created widespread uncertainty among trading partners. The European Union suspended ratification of a summer agreement, India deferred talks to finalize one, and the UK sought clarification on its negotiated 10% rate while keeping all options open. Countries are questioning the validity of concessions made—such as investment promises or eased business a

item.title: Diplomatic Stalemate as Russia Insists on Territorial Demands Despite Setbacks
item.description: Four years into the ongoing war in Ukraine, diplomatic efforts to reach a resolution remain slow and ineffective. Russian President Vladimir Putin continues to believe he can achieve victory, maintaining his goal of eliminating Ukraine as an independent nation, despite recent battlefield setbacks. Meanwhile, Ukrainians are skeptical of the negotiation process, particularly regarding the U.S. role, which they perceive as being influenced by unrealistic business deals proposed by Russia. Kirill Dmitriev, a key Russian financier, has promoted $14 trillion in potential deals if relations are restored, but the history of American investment in Russia, marked by corruption and legal risks, makes such promises highly dubious. Ukrainian officials are also critical of former U.S. President Donald Trump's stance, which they see as one-sided and pressuring Ukraine to make concessions, including the handover of the Donbas region without a realistic path for public approval. President Volodymyr Zel

item.title: Rob Jetten Sworn In as Netherlands’ Youngest and First Openly Gay Prime Minister Leading Minority Coalition
item.description:
Rob Jetten has been sworn in as the youngest prime minister in the history of the Netherlands and the country’s first openly gay premier. The 38-year-old leader of the Democrats 66 (D66) party secured a narrow electoral victory in October, defeating anti-Islam populist Geert Wilders in a closely contested race. Following the election, Jetten formed a centre-right minority coalition government with the People’s Party for Freedom and Democracy (VVD) and the Christian Democratic Alliance (CDA).
Because the coalition does not hold a parliamentary majority, the new government will need to negotiate support on a vote-by-vote basis in both houses of parliament to pass major reforms. Key elements of the coalition agreement include an additional €19 billion in defence spending, alongside significant cuts to healthcare and social benefits. The government also plans to tighten asylum policies by requiring refugees to apply for asylum outside Europe rather than after arrival, reflecting the sensi

item.title: France Seeks to Restrict US Envoy Charles Kushner’s Access After Dispute Over Extremism Comments
item.description:
A diplomatic dispute has emerged between France and the United States after comments posted by the US embassy in Paris sparked anger within the French government. The controversy began following the death of Quentin Deranque, a 23-year-old mathematics student who died after being assaulted by a group of masked individuals in Lyon. French ministers from the centre-right government attributed the attack to far-left militants. In response, the US embassy posted a statement on social media warning that “violent left-wing extremism is on the rise” in France.
French Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot strongly rejected the embassy’s remarks, accusing the US side of politicising a national tragedy. He stated that France did not require lessons from what he described as the “international reactionary movement” regarding issues of violence. Charles Kushner, the US envoy to France and father of Jared Kushner, was summoned to meet Barrot to clarify the comments. However, Kushner did not attend the

item.title: US Orders Partial Evacuation of Beirut Embassy Amid Heightened Tensions with Iran
item.description: The US government has ordered a partial evacuation of its embassy in Beirut, Lebanon, citing a security review amid escalating tensions with Iran. Approximately 50 embassy staff have been instructed to leave, with 32 staff and their families already flown out. The embassy remains operational with essential personnel. This decision follows heightened rhetoric from US President Donald Trump, who warned of consequences if Iran does not reach an agreement over its nuclear program. Iran has responded with threats of retaliation, and the US has continued to reinforce its military presence in the region, including deploying destroyers, fighter jets, and the aircraft carriers USS Gerald R Ford and USS Abraham Lincoln. US officials maintain diplomatic talks with Iran continue, but Trump has set an apparent 10-day timeframe to see whether an agreement is reached or military action will be taken. In parallel, anti-government protests in Iran have emerged, and Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s plan

item.title: England's Batting Woes Against Spin Threaten World Cup Progress
item.description: England's batting lineup continues to struggle against spin bowling in the ongoing T20 World Cup. Despite a 51-run victory over Sri Lanka, four of the top five England batters were dismissed by spinners, with Tom Banton run out. Pakistan, who use a higher percentage of spin than any other team, will pose a significant challenge in the upcoming match. Players like Sahibzada Farhan highlight Pakistan's strong spin options, including Usman Tariq, Mohammad Nawaz, Shadab Khan, and Abrar Ahmed. England's previous success against spin in bilateral series and T20s has not translated to the World Cup stage, with key players like Harry Brook and Jacob Bethell repeatedly falling to spinners. Brook averages just 19.1 against spin compared to 46.2 against pace, while Buttler has struggled for form. England's training focus ahead of the next match is limited, but the team hopes a fresh pitch in Pallekele will allow for more effective batting. England also possesses a capable spin attack themselves,

item.title: Italian Cricket Federation Faces Governance Turmoil Following Sexual Assault Allegation Against Senior Official
item.description:
Italian cricket is facing significant turmoil following the emergence of a sexual assault allegation against Prabath Ekneligoda, the women’s cricket coordinator of the Federazione Cricket Italiana (FCRI). The allegation, made to police in Rome in March last year by a member of the national women’s team, concerns an incident in which Ekneligoda allegedly inappropriately touched the player during a massage for a knee injury at a training session. The player, whose identity remains protected for legal reasons, stated she initially feared reporting the incident because she believed it could jeopardize her place in the team.
A criminal investigation concluded in November 2025, and Ekneligoda was interviewed by police the following month. Authorities are now determining whether there is sufficient evidence to proceed to trial. Ekneligoda denies the allegation, with his lawyer asserting that there are ulterior motives behind the claim and that a witness supports his account.
Although Ekneli

item.title: Human remains found in Sydney following mistaken identity kidnapping of 85-year-old man
item.description: Australian authorities have discovered human remains in connection with the kidnapping of 85-year-old widower Chris Baghsarian in North Ryde, Sydney. The elderly man was abducted just before dawn on 13 February, when CCTV footage showed masked individuals forcibly placing him into an SUV. Police believe the incident was a case of mistaken identity, with the actual target linked to organized crime. Earlier searches revealed a burnt-out vehicle containing a blood-stained carpet, which was linked to the alleged kidnappers and the abandoned property where Baghsarian was reportedly held. Videos circulating in Sydney's criminal underworld suggested the man was seriously injured. Police repeatedly appealed for his safe return, noting his health conditions and need for daily medication. New South Wales Premier Chris Minns suggested the kidnappers could return him to a public location or medical facility. Experts note that cases of mistaken identity are rising in Sydney's criminal networks, as

item.title: UK Parliament to Debate Release of Documents on Prince Andrew's Trade Envoy Role
item.description: The Liberal Democrats plan to push for the release of documents concerning Prince Andrew's appointment as a UK trade envoy, following his recent arrest. The party intends to table a motion in the House of Commons on Tuesday to compel the government to provide information about his role as 'special representative for international trade and investment' from 2001 to 2011, including vetting procedures. Andrew has been under scrutiny for his alleged links to the late financier Jeffrey Epstein, with released emails in the US suggesting he may have forwarded government reports and investment information to Epstein. While Andrew has denied wrongdoing, the Lib Dems argue that parliamentary scrutiny is necessary regardless of titles or connections. The debate will coincide with discussions in the business and trade committee about a potential parliamentary investigation into trade envoy appointments. The motion would be binding, similar to a recent Conservative effort regarding Lord Mandelson's

item.title: A Geological Exploration of Eight Remarkable Gemstones and Their Natural Origins
item.description:
The article explores eight extraordinary gemstones formed through natural geological processes over millions or even billions of years. It emphasizes that their beauty is not intentional, but rather the result of chemistry, pressure, heat, and time acting deep within the Earth’s crust. Labradorite from Canada is highlighted for its striking labradorescence, an optical phenomenon caused by light interacting with microscopic feldspar layers, producing shifting hues reminiscent of the aurora borealis. Myanmar’s rubies, especially those from the Mogok Valley, are prized for their intense “pigeon’s blood” red coloration, created by precise concentrations of chromium. Sri Lanka’s sapphires, formed in ancient metamorphic rock dating back to the Gondwana supercontinent, are renowned for their luminous cornflower-blue tone and clarity.
The article also examines malachite from the Democratic Republic of Congo, whose layered green bands form through copper-rich water deposits over long periods.

item.title: School Placement Delays Leave Many Children in Care Without Education for Months or Years
item.description:
A BBC investigation has found that many children living in care across England and Wales are missing months or even years of education because schools are refusing to offer them places. Despite legal requirements that looked-after children receive top priority in school admissions, children’s home providers report widespread difficulties securing placements, particularly for those with Education, Health and Care Plans (EHCPs). One 16-year-old boy, referred to as Tyler, has been rejected by 14 schools and has been completely out of school for 18 months, receiving only minimal educational support during his three years in care. His care providers say schools often claim they cannot meet the needs outlined in his EHCP, which include support for behavioural and trauma-related difficulties.
